description: Victory architecture redesign — territorial dominance + ideological alignment for all factions
decision: Deed checklist system dissolved for Crown/Church/Hafenmark/Varfell. Victory is now simultaneous conditions (TCV threshold + faction-specific requirements) held for 2 consecutive seasons. Conviction Track (CV) 0-5 per territory is the ideological axis contested by all factions. TC freezes at 75. Church shifts to territorial seizure post-75. TCV values assigned to all 17 territories (total 30). Crown Primary: TCV≥18 + Mandate≥5 + TC<50 + IP<60 + PI≥4 + moderate CV. Church Primary: TC≥75 → TCV≥18 with CV≥3 everywhere. Hafenmark Primary: TCV≥14 + Mandate≥4 + PI≥5 + Crown Mandate≤3 + reformed CV. Varfell: 3 paths retained with TCV integration. RM: 5 territories CV≤1 + RS≥40 + 5 Presence markers. Full spec in designs/board_game/victory_architecture_v1.md.
deprecation: Per-faction victory is NOT canon (Jordan 2026-06-06). The sole victory is peninsula control — eliminate all rival factions or subjugate them via diplomacy/treaties; one universal condition, not faction-specific. This TCV + faction-specific-requirements architecture is board-game-era and superseded; do NOT wire it into the sim. Referenced spec designs/board_game/victory_architecture_v1.md does not exist in the repo. Elimination/subjugation/treaty mechanics in ED-109/ED-304/ED-312 remain valid substrate for the single peninsula-control victory.

Wound mechanic drift correction. ED-694 (Vitality = Endurance × 10, Max Wounds eliminated, ED-202 floor preserved) introduced a linear simplification that did not match the WI × (Max Wounds + 1) total-damage-capacity structure for Endurance values 1, 2, 3, 5, 7 (only End=4 and End=6 happened to align numerically). Concurrent canon variously specified wound penalties as −1D Pool (Combat) vs +1 Ob (Thread, mass Command, BG CF tactic) creating channel inconsistency. PP-716 reverts ED-694 simplification: stat name Health, formula = (Endurance + 6) × (Max Wounds + 1), MW = floor(Endurance/2) + 1, WI = Endurance + 6. Universal wound penalty: −1D Pool, no Ob, ever. designs/scene/derived_stats_v30.md §4.1 made authoritative source. combat_v30, threadwork_v30, params/combat, params/mass_combat all reference §4.1 rather than restating. Cross-references: PP-716, ED-694 (superseded), PP-232.
source: Jordan canonical clarification 2026-05-09; contradictions surfaced during combat_arch_residual_stress_01 R1 module work
jordan_decision: 2026-05-09 — wounds = −1D Pool universally, never Ob; Health = (End+6)×(MW+1); MW = floor(End/2)+1; revert ED-694; derived_stats §4.1 authoritative; propagate everywhere.

description: [RESOLVED 2026-05-10] Founded faction starting stats — specified for Stage 4 emergence (settlement_layer §6.2). Resolution: emerged faction at Declaration receives the following starting stats: L 2 (limited recognition; advances via diplomacy + Conviction fulfillment), PS 3 (grass-roots base, anchored by founding NPC officers), I (Influence) = floor(Renown/2) at Declaration (carries Declaration roll pool forward), W = 2 + (# settlements controlled at Declaration − 1) capped at 5 (founding economic base + per-settlement scaling), Mil 1 (militia only at founding), Int 2 (informal network), Sta 3 (loyal officers, stable orgs). Faction stats advance from these baselines via standard Domain Actions and recovery mechanics (Mandate Recovery, etc.). Affected docs: settlement_layer_v30.md §6.2 — add Starting Stats subsection.
source: Sim audit 2026-05-10 (campaign walkthroughs v3, finding A1)
jordan_decision: 2026-05-10 — accepted as proposed. Implementation: add Starting Stats table to settlement_layer §6.2 in next propagation.
closure_commit: propagation: settlement_layer §6.2 - Founded Faction Starting Stats subsection added

description: [RESOLVED 2026-05-10] Treaty type qualification for §6.1 "Treaty-bound" effective hegemony. Resolution: Crown Treaty (victory_v30 §3.1), Capitulation (faction_layer §3.1), and Tributary (faction_layer §3.1) qualify as Treaty-bound for §6.1 effective hegemony purposes. Alliance, Peace, Commercial, and Truce do NOT qualify (parity relationships, not subordination). Rationale: §6.1 hegemony is about effective sovereignty over a territory's controlling faction — only subordination-treaties create that relationship. Affected docs: peninsular_strain_v30 §6.1 — add "Treaty-bound qualifying types" clarification; faction_layer_v30 §3.1 — add cross-reference to §6.1.
source: Sim audit 2026-05-10 (campaign walkthroughs v3, finding A3, E1)
jordan_decision: 2026-05-10 — accepted as proposed. Implementation: add §6.1 clarification + §3.1 cross-ref in next propagation.
closure_commit: propagation: peninsular_strain §6.1 - treaty qualification clarified, aligned with faction_layer §3 (Peace/Alliance/Capitulation/Tributary qualify per faction_layer existing canon)

description: [RESOLVED 2026-05-10] Generic Peace treaty territory cession — resulting Accord. Resolution: voluntary cession in ratified Peace treaty (Phase 3 Success or Overwhelming, mutual benefit clause): Accord set to 2 (legitimacy inherited from negotiated handover). Forced cession in Capitulation (dictated submission): Accord set to 1 (resentment of imposed terms, like military conquest). Tributary arrangement: territory remains under prior controller's direct rule (no Accord change at agreement; Tributary affects Wealth flow + §6.1 hegemony qualification only). Affected docs: peninsular_strain_v30 §2.3 — add Peace treaty cession row; faction_layer_v30 §3.1 — add Accord effects column to Treaty Types.
source: Sim audit 2026-05-10 (campaign walkthroughs v3, finding E11)
jordan_decision: 2026-05-10 — accepted as proposed. Implementation: add §2.3 row + §3.1 column in next propagation.
closure_commit: propagation: peninsular_strain §2.3 - Peace/Capitulation/Tributary cession rows added per faction_layer §3 existing canon

description: [RESOLVED 2026-05-10] Renown grant for Conviction resolution states. Resolution: Fulfilled +1 Renown (existing canonical), Transformed +1 Renown (transformation through play counts — Conviction evolved meaningfully), Failed +0 Renown (the attempt grants Momentum +1 per §2.3, not Renown — Failed is mechanically distinct from Fulfilled), Unresolved +0 Renown (no engagement). Rationale: Renown rewards public deeds and accomplished arcs, not internal struggle alone. Failed Convictions get Momentum (engaging the attempt) but not Renown (no public accomplishment). Transformed Convictions reflect substantive character evolution — public-facing enough for Renown. Affected docs: player_agency_v30 §5.4 Renown Sources table — expand "Conviction fulfilled" row to "Conviction resolved (Fulfilled or Transformed)".
source: Sim audit 2026-05-10 (campaign walkthroughs v3, finding E10)
jordan_decision: 2026-05-10 — accepted as proposed. Implementation: update §5.4 Renown table row.
closure_commit: propagation: player_agency §5.4 - Renown table: Fulfilled OR Transformed grants +1

description: [RESOLVED 2026-05-10] Govern action on freshly-acquired territory — Prosperity inheritance. Resolution: Prosperity inherits unchanged from prior controller at moment of conquest/transfer. Order in affected settlements drops −2 at conquest moment (already canonical per peninsular_strain §2.4b "battle at settlement −2 immediately"). Govern Ob computed from inherited Prosperity, +1 Ob from Accord 1 penalty (canonical §2). The Order penalty cascades upward to Accord recovery — restoring Order via Govern Success restores Accord progression. No additional Prosperity penalty needed; Order is the mechanical pressure point. Affected docs: peninsular_strain_v30 §2.4 — add clarifying note "Prosperity inherits; Order penalty per §2.4b governs recovery dynamics".
source: Sim audit 2026-05-10 (campaign walkthroughs v3, finding A6, E15)
jordan_decision: 2026-05-10 — accepted as proposed. Implementation: clarifying note in §2.4.
closure_commit: propagation: peninsular_strain §2.4c - Govern on Freshly-Acquired Territory subsection added

description: Volley Power-only pool ignores Size — massing ranged units provides zero additional firepower (Size 2 XBow rolls same 3D as Size 5 XBow). Inconsistent with Engagement pool where min(Size,Command) contributes dice. Removes strategic depth in ranged composition. Source: A2 sim F-006.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-11 — Yes. Size affects Volley. Pool = min(Size,Power)+Power dice TN6. Replaces Power-only.

description: Orderly withdrawal mechanics missing — mass_battle §A.12 covers Rout/Pursuit but no rule for orderly Withdraw battle plan vs Advance pursuers. Speed differential (Standard vs Slow) should permit disengagement but no canonical procedure. Source: A2 sim F-007.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-11 — Yes. Withdraw plan + Speed advantage (faster than pursuer) = orderly disengage, no engagement.

description: Rally action mechanical specifics unclear — mass_battle §A.7 Phase 6 step 4 lists Rally as general action but no canonical formula (pool, Ob, effect on Morale vs Discipline, success/failure consequences). Source: A2 sim F-008.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-11 — Rally pool = Morale dice TN7 Ob1. Success/Overwhelming = +1 Morale capped at starting value.

description: Strain vs Turmoil terminology inconsistency — phases.md §4d uses Strain; tracks.md uses Turmoil; peninsular_strain uses both. Confirm whether these are the same track, sub-components of same system, or distinct mechanics. Source: A2 sim F-010.

description: Field fortification mechanics missing — mass_battle §A.9 covers terrain modifiers but no rule for player-constructed fieldworks (earthworks, trenches, barricades). Historical precedent is decisive. Needed for siege approach scenarios. Source: A2 sim F-011.

description: Combined multi-unit attack resolution unspecified — mass_battle §A.7 allows max 3 engagements but does not specify how 2+ units attacking one defender resolves. Separate rolls? Combined pool? Defender dice allocation across attackers? Source: A2 sim F-012.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-11 — Combined attack via Fibonacci denominators. Lead full Off pool plus supporters floor(pool/Fib(n)). Denoms 2,3,5,8.

description: Gransol (T8) Fort level not specified in core.md territory table — T3/T14 are Fort 3, T10 is Fort 2, but T8 Hafenmark capital has no Fort entry. Lake geography provides natural defense but needs formalization. Source: A2 sim F-015.

description: Lake-city siege mechanics unresolved — Gransol (landlocked lake, Switzerland-analog) cannot be fully encircled. Does lake access prevent starvation siege? Can Crown blockade an inland lake? Distinct from naval Port-to-Port mechanics. Source: A2 sim F-016.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-11 — No. Lake supply routes not blockadeable. Inland lake is not naval.

description: Battle loss Stability trigger ambiguity — mass_battle Part E §E.1 says losing side Stab -1 immediate + §E.2 Stab -1 at Accounting = -2 per battle loss. Combined with territory loss trigger (-1), a single lost battle costs -3 Stability. At starting Stab 4, faction collapses in 2 battles. Verify this severity is intended — may need caps or graduated loss. Source: A2 sim F-017.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-11 — Defender -1 Stability only on territory loss. Attacker no penalty for failed capture.

description: core.md Faction Starting Stats table has Crown Military 4 and Lowenritter (Split) Military 6. Per Jordan 2026-05-11: Crown Military is 5, Lowenritter Military is 5. Additionally Lowenritter is not a separate faction at game start — it is Crown military arm. Lowenritter stats only apply IF Split occurs (not guaranteed). core.md table needs update.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-11 — Crown Mil 5, Lowenritter Mil 5. Lowenritter is Crown military arm until Split.

description: Lowenritter faction mechanics clarification per Jordan 2026-05-11. (1) Split outcome: Crown Military drops from 5 to 2 AND loses most units. (2) Coup outcome: Crown faction SUSPENDED until Lowenritter installs new monarch. If Torben/Elske/Lenneth = Crown continues. If Duke (Vaynard/Baralta) = Crown absorbed into that faction. (3) Lowenritter is NOT guaranteed to split or coup — only fires if they lose faith in Crown. core.md Graduated Autonomy table needs these outcomes formalized.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-11 — Split = Crown Mil 5->2 + unit loss. Coup = Crown suspended, monarch installed, faction follows monarch identity.

description: Engagement damage formula ambiguous. Phase 5 pool split does not specify whether damage = (net_successes * 1+Power) for attacker only, or (margin between off/def pools * 1+Power), or other. SIM-MB-04 INTERPRETATION-01 used margin model and produced one-turn kills via margins of 5-6. Needs explicit formula statement. Source: SIM-MB-04 FINDING-1.
jordan_decision: pending
stale_reason: Superseded — massbattle.py adopts PP-233 continuous net_successes×(1+power) at all sites; mass_combat.md Core Formula confirms; margin ambiguity resolved.

description: Volley post-ED-800 double-amplified. ED-800 approved min(Size,Power)+Power dice. SIM-MB-04 shows Power 3 unit goes 3 to 6 dice while multiplier 1+Power remains, producing average 21.6 dmg/turn vs units with 9-16 HP. Either pool or multiplier needs recalibration. Source: SIM-MB-04 FINDING-2.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-11 — Volley damage = max(0, net_successes) - ranged_DR. No (1+Power) multiplier. Composition (ED-814) handles scaling via back_pct × volley pool. Attrition contributor, not decisive.

description: ED-801 withdrawal phase gate unspecified. Jordan ruling confirmed Withdraw plan + speed advantage = orderly disengage. SIM-MB-04 implemented as Phase 5 gate but it may be a Phase 3 gate instead, changing positioning logic materially. Source: SIM-MB-04 FINDING-7.

description: Discipline redefined as formation coherence under fire — capacity to maintain designed shape, hold assigned column position, execute standing orders while taking casualties. Replaces vague 'organizational integrity' framing. Penalty table interpretation: pool penalty represents shape drift, not generic disorder. A high-Discipline unit holds the horseshoe even as the center bleeds; a low-Discipline unit reverts to local survival behavior — fighting whoever is in front, abandoning flank positions, ignoring orders. Aligns with historical doctrine (Vegetius De Re Militari, Maurice Strategikon, Leo VI Taktika). Triangulates with Command (tactical authority — what tactics are available) and Power (raw combat capability — how hard the unit hits) for clean three-stat unit quality model. Existing formulas (H per Size segment, penalty tiers 5-7/3-4/1-2, Reform Phase Discipline restoration) all remain valid under new framing.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-11 — Discipline = formation coherence under fire. Sharpens Power/Discipline/Command triangle.

description: Unit shape mechanical signatures. Five canonical shapes with empirically validated values from SIM-MB-05A/B/C (6000+ trials). Line (Disc 1, Cmd 1, no modifiers, baseline; drifts to mob). Arrowhead (Disc 4, Cmd 2, center column +2D Off, flanks -1D Off; tactical concept wedge breakthrough; hard counter GappedLine). Horseshoe (Disc 5, Cmd 3, center -2D Off +1D Def, flanks +2D Off whenever ANY engagement is resolved at the Horseshoe center column — positional H-2 trigger from SIM-MB-05C, 54 percent mean winrate; tactical concept Cannae envelopment per Hannibal 216 BC). GappedLine (Disc 5, Cmd 3, gap column empty cannot engage, non-gap columns +2D Off when opponent concentrates more than 40 percent front-row mass in gap column; tactical concept kill-zone channel per Roman precedent). RefusedFlank (Disc 3, Cmd 2, refused column -2D Off, engaged columns +1D Off compensating mass; tactical concept Epaminondas oblique order at Leuctra 371 BC). All shapes drift to Line on Discipline failure per ED-817. Player selects shape at battle setup per ED-820. Composition (ED-814) auto-distributes troops across the 9-cell grid according to shape template. Replaces existing Formation Types section: Wedge becomes Arrowhead preset; Shield Wall becomes Line+heavy front composition; Skirmish becomes spread-Line; Feigned Retreat becomes proto-Horseshoe.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-11 — Five shapes ratified per SIM-MB-05A/B/C empirical validation. Horseshoe trigger resolved as positional H-2 (any engagement at center fires flank trap), historically matching Cannae precedent. Horseshoe flank trap magnitude +2D Off per calibration sweep. GappedLine and RefusedFlank conditional/compensating mechanics validated.

description: Shape failure cascade — direct drift to Line model, validated by SIM-MB-05A/B/C. When unit Discipline drops below shape minimum, shape immediately becomes Line at Phase 6 Step 2 (after Discipline degradation check). No intermediate stages, no linear chain. Grid re-distributed using Line template. Drift is irreversible within battle. Players cannot voluntarily switch back to original shape mid-battle (ED-820 emergency reshape Command Ob 3 is the only mid-battle shape change mechanism). Tiered drift alternative tested in SIM-MB-05C and produces 5-15 percentage points better outcomes for sub-min Discipline units but adds significant design complexity (5 drift chains to specify, more state to track) without dramatic gameplay improvement; direct-to-Line preserves design clarity that Discipline gates shape selection. If preferring tiered drift later, ED-817 may be revised — but empirical case for direct is cleaner.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-11 — Direct drift to Line, irreversible mid-battle. SIM-MB-05C tiered alternative empirically tested and rejected for simplicity reasons.

description: Horseshoe trigger mechanism — RESOLVED via SIM-MB-05C branch exploration. Four trigger variants tested over 320 trials each: H-1 mass>40 percent (initial proposal) 37 percent mean winrate; H-2 positional always (fires when any engagement at center) 54 percent mean winrate; H-3 pool-ratio>40 percent 41 percent; H-4 weak-column AI heuristic 54 percent. H-2 selected as canonical: simplest mechanism, achieves balanced winrate target (45-60 percent across all opponent shapes), historically accurate (at Cannae Hannibal did not require Roman wedge formation, only Roman commitment to attacking his center). Horseshoe flank trap fires whenever any opposing unit's engagement is resolved at the Horseshoe's center column, regardless of opponent composition or shape. Closed into ED-816 specification.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-11 — H-2 positional trigger ratified. Trap fires on engagement at center, not opponent composition. Folded into ED-816 final spec.

description: Volley/Engagement composition balance. SIM-MB-05A composition sweep showed ranged-heavy compositions winning disproportionately (pure-melee vs ranged-heavy 4 to 1 disadvantage). SIM-MB-05C tested TN6 vs TN7 Volley calibration: TN7 improves pure-melee-vs-balanced from 28 percent to 36 percent (8 percentage point gain) but pure-melee-vs-ranged-heavy remains roughly unchanged (15-16 percent). TN7 is partial improvement but does not fully resolve. Recommendation: adopt TN7 as clean primary fix; raise composition-balance secondary measure as ED-825 for Phase 3+ work. Secondary measure options: a) melee_pct as engagement Ob bonus for heavier-melee side; b) Volley pool cap = floor(volley_pool / 2); c) ranged_DR scaled by defender melee_pct (heavier front rank shields back rank). Source: SIM-MB-05C section 3.2.
jordan_decision: pending
stale_reason: Superseded — Recommended TN7 NOT adopted — live VOLLEY_TN=6 + density-scaled volley + Lanchester; SIM-MB-05 concern superseded by re-architecture.

description: Combined attack effectiveness calibration. SIM-MB-05C 3v1 combined-attack test surfaced concern: Fibonacci-divided pool concentrated at one column may be LESS effective than 3 separate column engagements. Lead Power 4 Size 3 Arrowhead with 2 supporters concentrating at center: combined pool ~7 dice vs defender 1 column ~3 dice. Compared to distributed: 3 attackers engaging 3 columns each at ~3 dice vs defender split ~3 dice each. Distributed produces 3 simultaneous Successful engagements; combined produces 1 Successful engagement against full defender HP pool. ED-805 Fibonacci denominators (1,2,3,5,8) may be too aggressive. Resolution options: a) reduce denominators (1, 1.5, 2, 3, 5); b) combined attack damages all defender columns proportionally; c) combined attack with single column requires defender to have a single-column shape (e.g., Arrowhead) to be tactically meaningful. Test harness for 3v1 combined was incomplete in SIM-MB-05C; needs SIM-MB-06 rebuild. Severity P2, defer until after Phase 6 propagation.
jordan_decision: pending
stale_reason: Superseded — Live engine per-cell support-stacking + puncture, not Fibonacci denominators; 'needs SIM-MB-06' overtaken by tests/sim/mass_battle.

description: PP-717 D2 (Pool Softcap (terminology revision from "Pool Softcap" — was ambiguous with armour Damage Reduction) — combat-only diminishing returns above Agi 4) was sim-validated in v22-v27 weapon system audits and marked "ratified" in tests/audit/all_directions_ners_v27.md, but was NEVER propagated to params/combat. params/combat L14 has always carried the clean universal grammar `(Agi × 2) + H + 3`. The 2026-05-15 engine NERS audit incorrectly cited params/combat L14 as the source of the DR formula (F3/L-1) — citation was wrong; DR existed only in audit/sim layer documents. Jordan Decision-A 2026-05-15: PP-717 D2 explicitly REJECTED at canon layer. Combat pool retains universal grammar. Agi 6 pool-dominance gap (v27 sim showed 55% advantage at pool 17 vs 11) is queued for Phase 4 simulation re-check to confirm whether the problem PP-717 D2 was designed to address still manifests at current canonical values.

The MW cap is canonical in derived_stats §4.1 L57 but was never propagated to params/combat. At End 6: params says MW=4 (Health = 50 × 5 = 250 HP, wildly wrong); canon says MW=3 (Health = 48). Jordan to confirm whether to add to next sweep.

description: F13 (DISCOVERED during Phase 5 sim 2026-05-15 verification): params/core.md per-die EV table and probability quick-reference table both computed using face 10 = +1 success, NOT the canonical face 10 = +2 successes (per valoria_master_document §1.1, atom 03__1-1-die-face-rule-d10). Canonical values: TN 7 → E[net]/die = 0.40 (not 0.30); 10D pool → P(≥1) = 92% (not 99%). Engine code reading these tables computes wrong dice probabilities by 33%. Ratchet-shifts all downstream balance analyses (build matrices, Ob calibration) that referenced these tables. Also: stale "chain rule" note (≈ +1.43 EV) was flagged NON-CANONICAL by atom 03__1-1-die-face-rule-d10 in April 2026 but never removed from params/core.md. Both defects fixed in this commit.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-15 — fix in same commit as Decision E adoption.

description: Decision E 2026-05-15: continuous engine adopted as canonical specification for videogame (Godot) implementation. The engine is now specified two ways with statistically equivalent outputs: discrete (d10 dice, legacy/TTRPG mode) and continuous (Normal-distribution sampling, videogame mode). Continuous enables fractional Ob, fractional TN (already canonical for combat per PP-717 Fiore), and continuous degree resolution (outcome magnitude = net − Ob). Phase 5 sim 2026-05-15 validated equivalence (max deviation 0.029 in mean, 0.022 in std at pool sizes 5-17). Derived stat formulas, attribute scale, Ob scale, degree thresholds all unchanged. params/core.md §Continuous Engine is the canonical spec. derived_stats §1 acknowledges engine duality.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-15 — adopt continuous engine; canonical for videogame implementation.

description: Phase 7 sim 2026-05-15 (tests/sim/phase7_action_triangle_2026-05-15.md) demonstrated empirically that the Strike/Feint/Full Guard action triangle is a load-bearing combat balance mechanism. In Strike-only play, Fast (Agi 6, pool 17D) wins 96.7% conditional vs Strong (Agi 3, pool 11D); when Strong actively employs PP-294 Feint, Fast wins drop to 34.9% (Strong wins 65% of decisive duels). End-dominance (Tough vs Strong) similarly collapses from 82% Strike-only to ~7% with tactical play. Phase 4-7 chain conclusion: pool formula is NOT the structural driver of combat dominance; action triangle is. Decision A (revert PP-717 D2) vindicated; pool softcap was patching the wrong layer. Doubling decision becomes design-feel, not balance, choice. Documented in params/combat.md as Combat Balance Note section. Phase 8 with better-tuned Smart AI pending to confirm magnitude.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-15 — document Phase 4-7 finding as canonical balance note.

description: WS-H-4 resolution (workstream meta-audit 2026-05-15). Phase 7 End-dominance "Tough 82% Strike-only → ~7% with tactical play" result is likely a sim artifact: same-pool matchups (both 11D) with same Smart AI rules should produce similar outcomes between Strike-only and Smart modes, but the numbers shift dramatically. Most likely cause: Full Guard / Take Breath interaction in Smart mode that breaks the symmetric expectation. Combat Balance Note in params/combat.md previously canonized both the direction AND the magnitude; this edit retains the direction (tactical sensitivity exists) but defers the magnitude pending Phase 8 confirmation with better-tuned Smart AI. Agi-dominance findings (96.7% → 34.9% via underdog Feinting) are unaffected — they rest on the asymmetric Underdog Feints mode which is more trustworthy than Smart-vs-Smart.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-15 — execute REC-1 from post-audit recommendations.

description: WS-D-1 resolution (workstream meta-audit 2026-05-15). Decision E (continuous engine, ED-833) canonized the engine for all systems but Phase 5 sim validated only at combat parameters (pool 5-17D, TN 7). This is a documentation gap: the validation IS system-agnostic by Central Limit Theorem (Normal-approximation of d10 pool sums holds for any system using d10 dice), so the canonical scope is mathematically defensible. Edit adds explicit "Validation scope" paragraph to params/core.md §Continuous Engine acknowledging this — combat-validated; system-agnostic by CLT; faction-scale at small-pool boundary warrants dedicated sanity-check sim. No canonical change; clarification only.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-15 — execute REC-2 from post-audit recommendations.

Asymmetric verification weakens the audit trail and risks rare-case drift between local content and what landed on remote. description: Phase 8 sim (tests/sim/phase8_smart_ai_v2_2026-05-15.md) refines Phase 7 finding (ED-834): the "34.9% Feint inverts dominance" claim was partly stamina-management artifact. Phase 7 Underdog mode had Fast=strike_only (no Take Breath); Fast exhausted around round 5, losing many duels by stam exhaustion rather than damage. When both sides manage stamina via Smart v2 (Take Breath threshold ≤8 + PP-294-aware Feint alternation), Fast vs Strong reverts to ~100% Fast cond win; Tough vs Strong settles at 63.7% (real moderate End-dominance, not Phase 7 7.5% artifact). Calibration: symmetric Agi 3 vs Agi 3 produces 51.2% — sim balanced. Revised reading: pool advantage at canonical attribute differences is structurally significant in skilled play; the action triangle modifies but does not bridge a 6-die pool gap. Decision A (revert PP-717 D2) reopened pool dominance NOT compensated by action triangle alone. Combat Balance Note in params/combat.md updated with Phase 8 subsection. Whether to act on this (re-ratify PP-717 D2, drop doubling, pool cap, accept design) is a Jordan decision deferred.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-15 — proceed working; update Balance Note with Phase 8 refinement

description: Phase 10 sim (tests/sim/phase10_str_stam_reform_2026-05-16.md) tested two design-question reforms (canonical stamina formula, STR bonus dice on offense) and discovered a sim-side correction with retroactive implications. Primary finding (correction, not reform): Phase 4 through Phase 9 sims used Stamina = 15 + End * 2 (range 21-29) but canonical formula per ED-694 is Stamina = End * 5 (range 15-35) with Take Breath restoring partial stamina (End * 2 not full pool) and +1 stam cost per wound. Prior sims systematically underweighted Endurance. With canonical stamina, every End-investing matchup shifts 4-9pp from prior sim baseline; Fast vs Titan moves from 81.8% to 72.6% before any reform. Phase 8 finding ED-838 (Tough vs Strong 63.7% End-dominance) understates magnitude — direction unchanged, magnitude larger. Secondary finding (reform proposal, pending ratification): STR bonus dice floor(STR/3) on Strike and Disarm offense pool provides 2-6pp uplift to high-STR compensator builds. Best config under canonical stam + STR-strong: Fast vs Titan 67.5/32.5 — meaningful upset rate. Persistent finding: pool dominance survives all reforms. Fast (Agi 6) vs Titan (Agi 3 + max comp) finishes 70/30 even with undouble + 1/End Ob + Disarm + canonical stam + STR-strong; further levers (pool cap past Agi 4, static defense, smaller pools) untested. STR + light blade gap: Mighty (Agi 3, STR 7, light) sits at 98.5% loss to Fast — light weapon str_mult 1.0 + 2pp hit-rate gain from bonus dice cannot overcome 3-die pool gap. Either accept (STR builds wield heavy) or design specific light-weapon STR mechanics. Methodology corrections also recorded: TN vs Ob conflation in prior wound-penalty sims (Ob is net-success threshold not per-die TN shift); Disarm AI threshold bug fixed (opp_weapon_mod >= 3 not >= 4). Combat Balance Note in params/combat.md updated with Phase 10 subsection.
jordan_decision: 2026-05-16 — commit and proceed

description: Royal Progress Ob formula contradicts its own descriptive text. Part 10 §3.2 (Mode I — Royal Progress) canonical statement is: "Ob | floor(sum of own territories Accord / 2) — easier when Accord is high (people aren't restive); harder when realm is in turmoil". The formula floor(sum_accord/2) produces the OPPOSITE behavior from the descriptive clause: as sum_accord rises, Ob rises (action gets HARDER), not easier. Surfaced by M7 balance sweep N=1000 (commit d94f1e3e): Crown Royal Progress fails at game-start because starting sum_accord ≈ 30-33 across 6 Crown territories produces Ob 15-16 vs Pool = Influence (5) + Standing (0) = 5; mathematically unwinnable without modifier stacking. Possible resolutions: (a) revise formula to floor((max_possible - sum) / 2) where max_possible = 7 × num_owned_territories, making low-Accord realms HARDER not easier; (b) revise descriptive text to say "harder when Accord is high (king must show face to many contented subjects across long itineraries)" — but this contradicts the Henry I / Elizabeth I historical anchor; (c) accept the formula as-is, treat Royal Progress as a recovery-from-crisis action (low Accord → low Ob) and revise descriptive text accordingly. M6 implements as canon literally says (option c interpretation by default). Source: M7 balance sweep + Pass-3 finding #4.
jordan_decision: Option A ratified 2026-05-17 (Jordan directive earlier this session for iteration; formula matches descriptive text)
resolution:
Ob formula iterated to max(2, floor((sum_max - sum_current) / 2)) where
sum_max = 7 * num_owned_territories, sum_current = sum of own-territory
Accord values. Matches descriptive text ("easier when Accord is high").
Floor 2 prevents trivial auto-success at peak Accord. Cost (W -2),
Pool (I + Standing modifier), outcome table unchanged. part10 §3.2
amended with iteration note + verification table at illustrative Crown
states. Design intent clarified: Royal Progress is maintenance action,
not crisis recovery (use Coronation Renewal or Great Work for crisis).

description: Askeheim (T15) board game purpose
decision: Askeheim serves dual purpose: (1) narrative generator and major spoiler — if players do not engage with the Southernmost to help the Wardens, Rendering Stability (RS) trends to 0 and a second Calamity occurs; (2) Thread Sensitivity (TS) power/consequence nexus — increasing TS makes the practitioner increasingly powerful and influential but may accelerate RS decline if Thread operations are not judicious (Mending, careful use). The Southernmost is not dead space; it is the existential threat that gives RS its teeth.

description: Faction collapse cascade loops. L1 collapse loop terminates at Stability 0 = extinction (a termination, not a recoverable bound); L7 Wealth-0 -> Military-1/season is undamped on the Military dimension; decrease>increase asymmetry on Stability. Under canonical Ob = floor(stat/2)+1, the per-gate recovery odds are HEALTHIER than first reported (e.g. Stab-2 Accounting recovery ~0.26, not ~0.01), so the slide is clearly PROBABILISTIC and recoverable in the common case - NOT deterministic (earlier "deterministic" wording RETRACTED). Residual structural finding: the terminal state is still extinction (no bound short of it) and the Military-side cascade still lacks a damper. Fix: a bound short of extinction + a Military-side recovery path; lower priority than first assessed. RESOLVED-BOUNDED 2026-05-31: collapse loop bounded per Tainter / ners_historical_precedent_matrix - collapse = simplification + reconstitution (probabilistic, loss-weighted) with a recovery path, NOT terminal extinction (F6/F7 correction). faction_layer apply (designs/provincial) noted.

description: Mass battle has no Pool Floor; unit deletion (Size->0) serves as the implicit floor. Pool = min(Size,Command)+Command floors at 2D (Cmd-1, Size-1) - same degenerate math as ED-865 (P(>=3 | 2D) ~ 0.07), unlike personal combat (floor 5) and threadwork (floor 5). The unit-deletion-as-floor choice is architecturally legitimate but silent in canon and not player-legible. Options: explicit Pool Floor; document + UI surface; Pool Floor = Command. Jordan-decision; do not prescribe. RESOLVED 2026-05-31 (design-intent, vetoable): mass battle intentionally has NO Pool-Floor-5. Unlike personal combat (which protects individuals), mass-battle units are destructible and use unit-deletion (Size->0) as the implicit floor - a deliberate scale-difference (units routable/destroyable vs individuals protected) matching wargame convention. Lane C to validate the BALANCE (that units do not evaporate degenerately fast).

description: sigma-leverage engine Ob-floor (P-232) conflict: the engine applied advantage as an Ob reduction (eff_ob = base_Ob - eff_sigma*sigma_N), driving Effective Ob below 1 on ordinary favourable stacks -- violating canon P-232 (Obstacle Scale: Ob minimum 1; no modifier may reduce Ob below 1) -- and making the Overwhelming threshold (2*Eff_Ob) nonsensical when Ob went negative. Exposed F3 too: hardcoded SIGMA_N_COEFF=0.8 (TN7 sigma) miscalibrated impact magnitude off-TN7.
resolution: Reformulated advantage as a mu-shift (net_boost boosts the expected net: mean += eff_sigma*sigma*sqrt(N)); base_Ob and TN unchanged, so P-232 is never triggered and the degree ladder stays intact. Identical odds at TN7, TN-exact at TN6/8 (closes F3). eff_ob retained as an Ob-floored display helper only.
alternative_rejected: Exempting the sigma-transform from P-232 (a canon change) -- rejected: leaves F3 and the degree-ladder bug unsolved.

description: Faction Domain Action resolver — RATIFIED (Jordan 2026-05-31, 'works well'). At faction/strategic scale, bare-stat dice pools (1-7D) are statistically degenerate: a Military-2 faction succeeds at a Domain Action at only P~=0.070 @ Ob 3 (ER-1 / diagnostic F1; authoritative die rule, verified mu). Resolution: aggregate / deterministic+stochastic (sum relevant unit/derived values; CK3 / EU / Stellaris / KoDP / Six Ages precedent for acclaimed faction-scale resolution). ER-1/ER-2 + precedent INDEPENDENTLY justify the change (engine_replacement_audit L271); it does NOT rest on GD-2 (see ED-865 - GD-2 governs action-SELECTION only). SCOPE: faction/strategic layer only - the continuous graded resolver is KEPT at personal/provincial scale (repair-and-keep dominates at core, audit L290). Open prerequisite: F2 Ob-formula contradiction (ED-882-zone).

[GATE G8 - Jordan decision] Mass-battle low-Command sigma-leverage. Command sigma-leverage is HIGH at low Command (armature A6, computed): ~0.500/pt at Cmd 2, decaying to in-band at Cmd 4-7 - low-Command early-game generals get oversized per-point leverage (same small-pool sigma non-uniformity as ED-865/874, mass-battle instance). Closes attribute_weight_standard GAP #2. Candidate fix: apply the d+s resolver to mass-battle Command (per Stage-4, fixable only by deterministic+stochastic). PENDING Jordan: adopt the d+s fix, or accept low-Command leverage as intended early-game texture. | RESOLVED 2026-06-20 (Jordan resolve-all; mechanical-tier, vetoable): keep the high low-Command sigma-leverage as intended. Bottom-up [READ: orchestration.py base_combat_pool COMMAND_SIGMA]: pool=Command*(1+cohesion), so +1 Command is relatively larger at low Command (2->3=+33% base vs 6->7=+14%) -- command quality matters most where scarce. Top-down anchor: generalship historically had outsized effect on small/poorly-led forces (rout cascades under poor command); acclaimed strategy designs (Total War, Field of Glory) make leadership leverage high for small/elite units. COMMAND_SIGMA model stays as-is; magnitude defensible. Precision retune, if wanted, needs a sigma-sweep (out of scope).
source: faction diagnostic consolidated candidates; armature A6; faction handoff GATE G8
approved_by: Claude mechanical-tier (Jordan resolve-all 2026-06-20, vetoable)

description: General death as a consequence of mass battle eliminated — capture/incapacitation is the worst battlefield outcome for any named character; no mass-battle mechanic kills a player or named character.
decision: Reframed three sites in mass_battle_v30.md from death to incapacitation/capture: A.5 'General two-stage death'->'two-stage incapacitation' (Stage 2 killed->incapacitated/captured-if-field-lost; mechanical effect -2 morale/Command=0/uncommanded unchanged; general survives); A.4 morale triggers (General killed->incapacitated/captured; -2 + -5 max unchanged); D.2 officer death->incapacitation/capture (1d20<=Size-lost roll outcome reframed; companion 'combat death'->battlefield capture). Added a canonical 'No character death from mass battle' clause. The engine emits an 'incapacitated/captured' state + which side holds the field; the post-capture consequence (ransom/imprisonment/escape/return) is a world-layer event rendered OUTSIDE the mass-battle container (not designed here). Mechanical magnitudes/timing unchanged — fictional outcome only.

description: Canonical Size-based combat pool (min(Size,Command)+Command; derived_stats L276 / PP-233) set aside for the mass-battle engine resolution per Jordan canon-structure directive 2026-06-02. It made per-capita effectiveness size-dependent (degrades below Command), contaminating the Lanchester melee LINEAR law to an emergent exponent ~1.7 instead of 1. Command additionally redefined as a DERIVED stat.
decision: Mass-battle base exchange pool driven SOLELY by Command (sigma-leverage quality): pool = COMMAND_POOL_MULT*Command (=2*Command, the canonical value at Size>=Command); numbers (Size) enter outcomes ONLY via the Lanchester frontage term. Command DERIVED = clamp(round((2*Charisma + Cognition)/3), 1, 7) -- Charisma primary weight, Cognition secondary. Validated by conserved-quantity exponent (no-rout primitive): melee restored to true Lanchester LINEAR (p~1.0, conserves A-B), volley stays SQUARE (p~2.0), coefficient-invariant (K sets timescale not law); magnitude historical (winner-low/loser-high per du Picq victors <5%, no-annihilation rout-decided, square concentration 17.8x Cannae/Pharsalus). Gated COMMAND_SIGMA_ENABLED (default ON; OFF byte-exact to the prior canonical pool, verified isolated). Committed 2cf3feb6. Constants COMMAND_POOL_MULT=2 / CMD_CHA_WEIGHT=2 / CMD_COG_WEIGHT=1 class-B sim-tunable, Jordan-confirmed. FOLLOW-UP (not yet applied): canon DOC reconciliation -- params/mass_combat.md PP-233 pool + derived_stats Command derivation; engine leads, docs to follow.

description: RATIFY + CANONIZE all 2026-06-05 mass-battle session work per Jordan ('ratify, canonize, commit all'). Engine-side (tests/sim/mass_battle/orchestration.py). (a) DISCIPLINE DECOUPLED FROM MOVEMENT SPEED (afdabadf): the disc_mult-on-actual_speed/step coupling was engine-introduced with no canonical basis; PP-232 renames Cohesion->Discipline and discipline IS cohesion (Power penalty A.4, degradation under Size-loss asymmetry, Reform restoration, H formula); params/mass_combat.md has ZERO discipline x movement coupling. Coupling removed (actual_speed = base + stance_mod); disc_mult retained only where canonical (node cohesion factor k, wheel rate kw). (b) reform_check WIRED (8ec085c0) + CANON-CORRECTED (34db3c91): empty G-8 hook now restores +1 discipline toward discipline_start for a unit not in melee contact, gated by the full L180-183 rule -- general's Command >= current Discipline + 1 AND Command >= 2 (PP-241); Command=1 cannot restore; Command caps reformable discipline. (c) DIRECTED MANEUVER PATHING (8ec085c0): units no longer always path straight at nearest. envelop = go wide past the enemy's nearer flank then hook into its flank/rear; IMPLEMENTS the existing canonical Envelopment tactic (A.8) and extends the canonical wing-pathing precedent (A.3b L125 Horseshoe). surge = break a line and drive deep past the enemy front (penetration); gap-fill, no prior canonical tactic. Subunit.maneuver + maneuver_side, also fired by instructions tokens (Flanker envelop template now live); _maneuver_target stateless per-tick; advance_cells direct column-local+wheel path is byte-exact default. (d) TARGETING GRANULARITY CONFIRMED unit->subunit->cell. (e) PC_NODE_COHESION continuous-path KeyError FIXED (545af082) + self-healing fabrication gate / ledger auto-bootstrap (88479e58); infra.
decision: All canonical. Discipline = cohesion (PP-232), NOT a movement-speed input -- disc_mult-on-speed permanently struck. reform_check is the canonical L180-183 reform with the PP-241 command gate. envelop is the engine implementation of the A.8 Envelopment tactic; surge is a canonical class-B breakthrough/penetration maneuver (gap-fill). Maneuver constants MANEUVER_SURGE_DEPTH=5, MANEUVER_ENVELOP_WIDE=4, REFORM_ENGAGE_DIST=1 are class-B sim-tunable (ratified, Jordan-vetoable). Targeting granularity unit->subunit->cell is the confirmed canonical structure.
top_down_anchors: envelop: Hannibal double envelopment at Cannae (216 BC); Total War directed flanking. surge: Theban deep column breaking the Spartan line at Leuctra (371 BC); Napoleonic ordre-mixte breakthrough column; operational-wargame breakthrough mechanics. reform: standard cohesion-recovery-when-disengaged in operational wargaming.
bottom_up_sources: engine assign_targets + advance_cells (column-local + PC_WHEEL overhang) traced; maneuvers extend the existing per-cell pather. canon: mass_battle_v30.md A.8 Envelopment, A.3b L125 wing pathing, L180-183 reform / PP-241, L7 PP-232 rename; params/mass_combat.md (zero speed coupling).
validation: counters deepColumn 5/8 ShieldWall 8/8 command 8/8 byte-identical across all changes; fuzz(120) 0 engfail/0 degen; reform cmd5/d3->4, cmd4/d4 no-change, cmd1/cmd2 closed; envelop hooks flank (lateral 7 > flank 5, row 17), surge breaks weak line (row 10 vs direct 16).

description: APPLY ratified de-saturation + venue bar-balancing pass to the ground-up contest engine (designs/audit/2026-06-03-contest-groundup/). (a) DE-SATURATION: engine.OVERWHELM_SIGMA=0.85; degree() gains an optional pool arg -> live Overwhelming bar = MU_PER_DIE*pool + 0.85*SD_PER_DIE*sqrt(pool), so the degree-3 rate stays bounded across pool sizes (pool-8 0.44->0.15) instead of saturating; pool-less degree(net,ob) legacy path UNCHANGED so direct-call unit tests keep meaning, only _reception takes the gated bar. (b) RES_FLOOR=0.15 in resolver: a hostile/off-axis reception can't zero an advance's resonance (raw resonance dips to 0.097 at secret_council/pathos, so the floor binds). (c) WIN-CONDITION BAR SWAPS in modes.py: court ProofBar 4.0->2.0, inquisition 3.0->2.5, excommunication 5.0->3.0, appeal Grace 7.0->5.0, memorial 6.0->5.5, imperial Grace 8.0->5.5. (d) BALLOTTA: secret_council ThresholdRace(3.0) -> VoteAtClose(jurors=5, sharpness=0.6, noise=0.8), un-fusing the verdict from momentum (ROUT 62%->38%, SPLIT_DECISION now reachable 0.11). (e) NEW counterpuncher policy: confirmatio-then-refutatio (build first half, rebut the opponent's lead in the back half when behind); first draft was a dominated pure-rebut policy (lost 26-374), redesigned to viable (0.60-0.74) and the strongest drama generator. (f) tests re-baselined (skill-gap decisive 0.6->0.25, overwhelming-succession 0.5->0.20; both fell because de-saturation moved mass to committee/split while preserving the skill tilt A~12:1 over B), secret_council assertion -> VoteAtClose, 5 stale cross-cultural labels corrected, +5 new regression checks. 151/151 unit tests green. HELD (NOT in this ratification): imperial sovereign discipline 0.05->0.25 -- handoff lists it in both the ratified-apply and Jordan-owned sets with a third value (0.20) in evidence; left at 0.05 pending Jordan; the imperial GraceThreshold swap was applied separately. FINDINGS for Jordan (not changed): drama floor missed by the five asymmetric bar/grace venues (intrinsic to threshold dynamics, not a tuning bug); imperial & memorial courtier-dominated 0.92/0.99 with logos-family at 0% (discipline lever does not fix -- venue ethos-weighting does); counterpuncher cannot see venue.allow_rebuttal (ContestView gap) so self-destructs in no-rebuttal venues; 7v1 succession ~73% split (faction-layer legibility, sec 7.2.1); narrative.py stale 'SPLIT only under VoteAtClose' comment. P8 venue-doc citation hygiene remains a follow-up.
decision: De-saturation (OVERWHELM_SIGMA, pool-aware degree-3), RES_FLOOR, the six bar swaps, the secret_council ballotta, and the counterpuncher policy are applied and verified (class-B sim-tunable, Jordan-vetoable). Imperial sovereign discipline stays 0.05 (held). The drama-floor shortfall on asymmetric venues and the imperial/memorial courtier monoculture are surfaced as Jordan design calls, NOT ratification blockers.
top_down_anchors: counterpuncher: Quintilian, Institutio Oratoria -- partes orationis, confirmatio before refutatio (prove your case, then rebut). ballotta: Venetian/Athenian secret-ballot courts severing the counted verdict from the room (dikasteria; thorubos vs the vote). de-saturation: contest-engine diagnostic Lesson 2; RES_FLOOR: Lesson 6.

Chronicle re-measured N=80/ordered-pair 6-policy round-robin 5v5 + asymmetric accuser sweeps N=600.
validation: 151/151 unit tests; chronicle: no shape >50% (max inquisition ROUT 0.44), COLLAPSE 0.67-1.0 with fault-prone policies, >=2 viable approaches in 8/10 venues; excommunication ethos-accuser 0.02->0.37 at bar 3.0; counterpuncher zero-fault/zero-exhaustion in rebuttal venues; de-saturation pool-8 degree-3 0.44->0.15; res-floor min raw resonance 0.097<0.15.

title: FM three-level command architecture (mass battle, videogame)
description: Football-Manager-style three-level compositional command architecture for mass battle (videogame realization), adopted across design conversations 2026-05-15 (FM as canonical command reference; mentality > team instructions > roles > player instructions; historical-general / Cannae model) and 2026-06-02 (allocation grid + team-instruction layer + intervention windows). LEVELS: (1) Army doctrine [Unit] - general sets doctrine plus a team-instruction layer: Aggression {Cautious, Balanced, Pressing} and Cohesion-priority {Hold shape, Adapt}, conditioning how subordinate formations behave under stress. (2) Unit formation [Subunit] - each company adopts a formation from a default template OR via the allocation grid, an adjustable allocation surface where the player sets intent (e.g. '60% melee, horseshoe, hold defensive') and the system auto-distributes, with manual per-cell override ('the FM analogy is exact'). (3) Subunit role/subformation - company-internal geometry plus assigned role. CADENCE: deploy formations -> set doctrine -> assign units to roles -> ~5-20 intervention windows per battle (set intent, watch, intervene at punctuated decision points; not per-tick control). NOTE: 'ED-814', the label by which this was informally cited, was never a real ledger entry; this entry replaces it. Provenance reconstructed from conversations; fidelity confirmation invited (Jordan instructed filing 2026-06-09). | RATIFIED 2026-06-20 (Jordan accept/ratify/resolve-all): fidelity confirmed -- Jordan's FM three-level restatement (army strategy/formation -> sub-unit troop-type+subformation+tactic -> each cell inherits best tactic-execution) matches this filing. Engine realization: L1 Aggression(stance), formation geometry, L3 roles/targeting, per-cell discipline inheritance, instructions present; player-control layers (L1 Cohesion-priority {Hold-shape/Adapt}, L2 allocation grid, intervention-window cadence) deferred to videogame-UI/future (not gaps); per-cell execution-layer drift-orphaning defect fixed as ED-1032 (46373da8).

chat 2026-06-02 Bottom-up cavalry combat gauge implementation

title: Echelon mapping + formation/subformation distinction
description: Echelon mapping and formation/subformation distinction (Jordan ruling, 2026-06-09 session). ECHELON MAPPING (three echelons, not four): battalion = army = Unit (the general's whole force; carries doctrine and the allocation grid per ED-907); company = Subunit (carries the formation/subformation); squad = cell/troops (emergent). FORMATION vs SUBFORMATION: a subformation is company-internal geometry, realized as Subunit.shape (a single body's arrangement; bottom-up). PLACEMENT (per ED-909): the Unit-level strategic postures (Envelopment, Refused Flank) are NOT Subunit shapes; they are army-level and emerge from sub-unit positions plus assigned flanking roles, realized as allocation-grid deployment presets, not maintained shapes. Company-level (Subunit) declarable geometries are Line, Arrowhead (wedge), and GappedLine.

title: Formation-taxonomy reconciliation (echelon split + terminology); supersedes ED-816 in part
description: Reconciliation of the two formation taxonomies at the echelon level (Jordan adjudication 2026-06-09, resolving options a+b+c). (a) SUPERSEDE: ED-816 canonized five shapes (Line, Arrowhead, Horseshoe, GappedLine, RefusedFlank) as empirically-validated sub-unit shapes; Horseshoe and RefusedFlank are removed from the Subunit shape set. Engine re-architecture required (they leave Subunit.shape) - approved as part of a known-needed engine revamp. (b) SPLIT, historically validated: Unit-level (army deployments, multi-body, emergent) = Envelopment [double envelopment; precedent Cannae 216 BC, Hannibal - center yields concave while wings + cavalry wrap flank and rear] and Refused Flank [oblique/refused order; precedent Leuctra 371 BC, Epaminondas; Leuthen 1757, Frederick the Great]. Sub-unit-level (single-body geometries) = Line [basic fighting line], Arrowhead/Wedge [cuneus / breakthrough], GappedLine [open/gapped order; the ED-816 wedge-counter; cf. Roman manipular quincunx gaps]. (c) TERMINOLOGY: 'Horseshoe' and 'RefusedFlank' are retired as Subunit.shape names. 'Envelopment' (army-level emergent posture, subsuming colloquial 'horseshoe'/'double envelopment') and 'Refused Flank' (army-level deployment) are reserved for the Unit level - matching the existing mass_battle_v30 tactics of those names. A company whose cells curve concave is exhibiting emergent wing-pathing, not a declared shape. RESIDUAL (still to detail): the full mapping between the A.6 dice-modifier formations (Shield Wall, Wedge, Skirmish, Column, Feigned Retreat, Reserve) and the geometric shapes - working hypothesis is orthogonal axes (geometry = Subunit.shape x posture = A.6 dice stance), e.g. Shield Wall = a Line geometry in a defensive posture; Wedge = Arrowhead geometry in an offensive posture. Flagged for a follow-up decision. FOLLOW-UP: ED-816 to be amended and the engine re-architected (Horseshoe/RefusedFlank Subunit.shape -> Unit-level emergent presets) as part of the revamp.
citations: designs/provincial/mass_battle_v30.md A.6 FORMATION TYPES; Envelopment/Refused Flank tactics
historical precedent: Cannae 216 BC; Leuctra 371 BC; Leuthen 1757

title: Phase-dependence finding: FM26 IP/OOP does not map to melee (open)
description: Phase-dependence finding (open; Jordan decision pending). FM26's In-Possession/Out-of-Possession two-shape model does NOT map cleanly to Valoria mass-battle melee. Disanalogy: football possession is exclusive (a clean binary), whereas Valoria melee is simultaneous mutual offence/defence - per A.7 each sub-unit declares an Offence/Defence pool split in Phase 1 (any allocation, min 1D each, secret, revealed Phase 5) and at Phase 5 Engagement both sides roll offence AND defence at once; a unit is never purely 'in' or 'out of possession'. The offensive/defensive control FM's split would add already exists: the per-turn Off/Def pool split (a continuous dial) + the formation posture (Wedge offensive / Shield Wall defensive) + Prepared Defence, all re-declarable at the ~5-20 intervention windows. The only genuinely new element is within-moment situational switching (auto-adopting a defensive posture when charged/flanked) - and the surprise-charge gap that addresses is already punished by the per-cell flank/fixed-by-other mechanics and mitigated by declaring Shield Wall in anticipation. RECOMMENDATION: do NOT import the two-formation IP/OOP split; if playtesting shows a real surprise-charge pain point, add only a light reactive-defensive-posture (a 'form to receive' reaction built from existing Shield Wall / Prepared Defence primitives) - playtest-gated, not built now. Grounds: NERS-Elegant, the over-engineering guard, and the Total-War 'no plethora of toggles' lesson. | RESOLVED 2026-06-20 (follows ED-907 ratification; vetoable): FM IP/OOP two-shape possession model is NOT adopted for Valoria melee -- structural disanalogy stands (football possession exclusive/binary; melee simultaneous mutual offence/defence). The adopted FM analogy is the tactical-command hierarchy (ED-907), orthogonal to IP/OOP. Settled consistent with Jordan's ED-907 ratification.
citations: designs/provincial/mass_battle_v30.md A.7 (Off/Def pool split; Phase 5 simultaneous offence+defence)

title: P1 supersession-scope gap: ranged / group / thread-in-combat have no engine resolution (open — Jordan)
description: Comprehensive personal-combat audit 2026-06-09 (designs/audit/2026-06-09-personal-combat-comprehensive/comprehensive_analysis_personal_combat.md, F1). combat_engine_v1 is ratified as THE canonical personal-combat resolver superseding the RESOLUTION layer of combat_v30 (ED-900/ED-904), but the engine resolves strictly 1v1 melee. Ranged combat (combat_v30 §5: LP TN7 / HP TN6 / sling TN8, ranged DR table, PP-720 friendly fire), group combat >2 (§8: zone collapse, Fibonacci offence bonus PP-216, rescue, multi-engagement), and thread-in-combat (§10) exist ONLY in the superseded resolution layer with no engine counterpart. canonical_sources wording vs the 2026-06-02 ratification proposal diverge on whether these are retained or orphaned. DECISION (Jordan): (a) declare them RETAINED-canonical on the combat_v30 dice chassis pending engine extension — annotate canonical_sources + combat_v30 header to declare the two-resolver seam; or (b) scope an engine extension. Until adjudicated, personal combat is incomplete at system scope (NERS-R fail in the audit verdict).
OWN-5 defection cascade (L-DEFECT, B1.2/B1.3) resolution. Was UNBUILT (loop_safety_ledger FAIL NERS-R). Jordan delegated best-judgment 2026-06-09.
decision: GAP-FILL RESOLVED (vetoable; Jordan delegated 'best judgment' 2026-06-09). OWN-5 = L-DEFECT relational defection cascade (B1.2/B1.3), marked UNBUILT in loop_safety_ledger (FAIL NERS-R, section-7 hooks only). DECISION: build as specced with an explicit fragility-multiplier (loop gain) + suppression-brake (player damper), sized so the loop is BOTH damped and bounded (resolution-diagnostic Lesson 5). MECHANIC: (a) TRIGGER relational edge-break (loyalty severed by defeat/betrayal/faction-weakening); (b) FRAGILITY-MULTIPLIER each completed defection raises faction fragility, lowering remaining members' defection threshold (the positive-feedback gain); (c) SPILLOVER cascade reaches relational neighbours at 0.5 strength/hop (geometric 0.5^hop -> bounded reach) [DAMPER, spatial]; (d) STRAIN damper -1/s (reuse DC-8 strain, gain-verified mean-reverting); (e) SUPPRESSION-BRAKE holder's Suppress action = -1 to the defection check per suppression, bounded (mirrors ED-853 insurgency suppression) [DAMPER, player]; (f) CAP tier-3 (mass defection) rare by threshold; cascade bounded by finite membership + strain-tier ceiling. Net per-cycle gain <1 via (c)+(d)+(e); bounded via (f) -> passes Lesson 5. BOTTOM-UP: loop_safety_ledger L-DEFECT (KOEI model; strain -1/s, 1/2 spillover, tier-3 rare); npc_relational_graph_v30 section-7 hooks; ED-853 suppression sibling. TOP-DOWN: published design (KOEI RTK/Nobunaga loyalty-defection; Paradox CK vassal-defection cascades w/ liege suppression brake) + threshold-cascade theory (Granovetter threshold model; Kuran preference-falsification, 1989 Eastern Bloc - each visible defection lowers the next holdout's threshold = the fragility-multiplier). BUILD STAGED (not landed here): author into npc_relational_graph_v30 section-7 / political_hierarchy B1.2-B1.3 as a PP; magnitudes sim-tunable. Closes OWN-5 / loop-safety section-9 step 3 (decision); roadmap pending -> resolved.

decision: Built per ED-1000: tier-laddered cascade + hop-attenuated 1/2-per-hop spillover (section-6.2) + Fragility-multiplier (gain, cap +3, -1/s decay) + Suppression-brake (-1/check, ED-853 sibling) + tier-3 hard cap. Loop damped+bounded (resolution-diagnostic Lesson 5). canonical_sources SHA refreshed (co-file). SIM-DEFECT (Lane C) testing PENDING: magnitudes (1/2-per-hop, Fragility +1/cap+3, sever-DC coupling, tier-3 trigger=3, Suppress -1) are illustrative and the per-cycle-gain bound is a design argument, NOT yet sim-measured; do not treat magnitudes as load-bearing until SIM-DEFECT confirms cascade termination.

title: Fieldwork canonical-line split: Disposition / Knot / Ob-application (3-way)
description: Three live docs carry incompatible specs. Disposition range/ceiling: -3..+5 ceiling=Bonds (fieldwork_v30 §5.1, PP-684 line) vs -4..Bonds direct-subtraction with +4-capped label table and stale floor(Bonds/2)+1 lifepath clamp (params/fieldwork, PP-632, internally self-contradictory) vs -4..floor(Bonds/2)+1 (investigation_systems_v30 Ontological Ledger). Disposition->Ob: stepped table caps ±3/-2 (master §5.1) vs effective Ob=max(1,baseOb-Disposition) direct subtraction (params, PP-632) — divergent at |Disp|>=2. Knot: strain model Spirit×2 TN7 Ob2, tiers Distant/Close cap 4/7, prereqs Disp+5+TS>=30+Bonds>=5 (§5.6a/b, ED-773/ED-780) vs tier-cost model any character, pool (Bonds×2)+3, tiers Loose/Medium/Close 1/2/5 (params, PP-632). Master §5.6b cites nonexistent §3.5 and resets rupture to -4, below its own -3 floor. Blocks Lane-C module 20 port.
source: Fieldwork full-depth diagnostic (audit ef659454b0c8; commit a11e2fa; 2026-06-10), staged in designs/audit/2026-06-10-investigation-exploration-diagnostic/ledger_candidates_jsonl_ready.jsonl; filed by Lane A 2026-06-11 (LA-1)
jordan_decision: Pick canonical line (master fieldwork_v30 recommended per 2026-06-04 attributes F-DISP 'ceiling=Bonds'); decide stepped-table vs direct-subtraction Ob rule; decide -3 vs -4 Disposition floor; decide Knot model (strain ED-773 vs PP-632 tier-cost). Then regenerate params/fieldwork + splits.
resolution: RESOLVED (Jordan ruling, 2026-06-28; commit 75a3eb9/a3837d0). Disposition = flat −5..+5 (no Bonds ceiling; Bonds ≥ 5 is now an explicit Knot prerequisite), resolved via a stepped Ob table; Knot model = ED-773 strain reframed as a bidirectional −5..+5 bond-strain gauge (Distant −2..+5 start 0; Close −5..+5 start −2; −5 = Tempered); break/rupture = Disposition −3, 4 Composure. Source-of-truth + consumer tail regenerated; supersedes PP-632/PP-684. Closes the 3-way canonical-line split.

title: PP-236 'Crown has no Intel stat' section unmarked-superseded by ED-787 in stats_1_7_scale.md
description: Intra-file canonical contradiction in params/factions/stats_1_7_scale.md. The PP-236 section '## Crown Covert Actions (PP-236) [PROVISIONAL - pre-ledger, accepted as canonical per 2026-04-26 audit]' states 'Crown has NO Intel stat. Crown covert actions (investigation, sabotage, intelligence gathering) use the Influence pool at +1 Ob.' This is superseded but UNMARKED: ED-787 (CLOSED 2026-05-03, Jordan signoff, Position A) restored Intel as a canonical faction axis and the same file's Starting Stats table gives Crown Int 3, with the 'Mechanical roles for Intel' block stating 'Offensive intelligence quality: Investigate/Intel pool = Intel (not flat Influence as fallback)'. So the PP-236 Influence-fallback for Crown covert is overturned by ED-787, but its prose remains live (marked only PROVISIONAL, not superseded). Implementation risk: an implementer reading the PP-236 section would roll Crown covert as Influence+1Ob, contradicting the decided Crown Int 3 / Intel-pool rule. SCOPE: only the 'Crown has no Intel stat / Influence+1Ob' claim conflicts; the adjacent Crown->Loewenritter Intel-delegation rule (PP-241, line ~370) and Loewenritter reactive-military NPC guidance (PP-238, line ~382) are separate and may stand. Surfaced during LA-17 faction propagation-debt investigation (this session).
