description: Contest rebuild Stage 3 / Gate C (rhetoric grounding + adjudicator armature) FINAL ratification (Jordan, 2026-07-02). The full Stage 3 exploration (4 rounds, converged, judge findings 1/4/5 fixed, 1041+377 tests green, uncommitted) surfaced 4 open decisions; Jordan ratified all 4 via AskUserQuestion -- filed here DIRECTLY as ratified (not provisional-then-flipped, since the AskUserQuestion answers ARE the ratification, unlike ED-1055/1056/1057-1061 which were filed provisional pending a later Jordan pass). DECISION 1 -- ARMATURE 4TH AXIS: RATIFIED -- keep Insinuation as the armature's own 4th axis. The adjudicator-armature dot-product basis stays {Evidence, Consequence, Authority, Insinuation}. Insinuation is a DELIBERATE NEW 4th axis authored specifically for the third-party-adjudicator mechanism, NOT a claim to reuse canon's actual 4th Resonant-Style type (Solidarity -- npc_behavior_v30.md:39, relational/Knot-gated: 'Any genre + Revealing; requires active Knot'). Solidarity presumes an active Knot between the two orators in the exchange, which does not fit a third-party judge/crowd/panel who need not be Knot-bound to either side; Insinuation instead names what a third party CAN plausibly be moved by -- the implied/unstated register (inference rather than open proof), needing no relational precondition. Numeric weights (STYLE_AXIS_PRIMARY=1.0, STYLE_AXIS_OFFAXIS=0.15) remain [SEED] pending future PP-674 vetting + sim-calibration -- NOT separately blocked by this ratification (the ratification settles the STRUCTURE question only). DECISION 2 -- EPIDEICTIC COMPRESSION: RATIFIED -- accept the 2-genre compression as-is. The 2-genre (Memory/Projection) grid (PP-234, 3->2) stays; Aristotle's third genre (epideictic, present-tense praise/blame) survives ONLY via the ethos_present/praise-blame register (RhetoricalWeights *_present column, live in resolver._advance's joint_weight; the basilikos-logos ceremonial register), NOT as a first-class genre with its own primary-genre bonus. No code/prose mechanic change was needed beyond recording this ratification -- restoring a third EPIDEICTIC genre (cost: a 5th/6th style-card, breaks the clean 2x2 Genre x Orientation style grid) was considered and rejected. DECISION 3 -- CR5 SCOPE: RATIFIED -- keep the Doubt Marker (landed Obscuring, ED-1060) and the self-Face backfire (failed Obscuring, this stage) TOGETHER as the full CR5 realization, not two conflicting mechanics. A landed Obscuring move produces the Gate-B Doubt Marker (opponent-facing, a track-margin penalty on the marked side's next/terminal winning exchange); a FAILED Obscuring move (reception degree 0 -- a genuine nigrahasthana/foul that lands nowhere) now ALSO costs the mover's own Face (min(2, own current Face), the Doubt Marker's -2 as a magnitude precedent, standing-bounded per the ratified CR5 carry-across 'gated by SelfGating.licit'). The separate 'opponent-Face-attack' idea (a landed Obscuring move stripping the OPPONENT's Face stat directly, which would retire the Doubt Marker) is NOT pursued -- the Doubt Marker already fills that landed-Obscuring-move role, so a second attack mechanism would double up rather than complete CR5. No mechanic change was needed for this decision beyond recording the ratification (the self-Face backfire was already wired in the Stage 3 exploration; this decision closes the design question of whether it conflicts with the Doubt Marker -- it does not). DECISION 4 -- CR4 REACHABILITY (THE REAL WORK, code change): RATIFIED + IMPLEMENTED -- fixed the reachability gap. All 8 canonical proceedings previously started at the QUALITY (qualitative) stasis rung (resolver.Venue.start_ground default); stasis only ever shifts UPWARD during play (resolver._advance gates on Stasis.stronger_than, and the Stasis.LADDER order is FACT < DEFINITION < QUALITY < JURISDICTION < CONSEQUENCE < FEASIBILITY); so the conjectural FACT stasis -- the ground rhetoric.STASIS_PRIMARY_GENRE maps to Memory, the ONLY ground that sets Memory primary -- could never be reached in any shipped proceeding, so a Precedent/Suppression (Memory-chosen) orator's CR4 +1D primary-genre bonus was structurally dead in real play (reachable only via a synthetic test Venue built outside the canonical registry). VERIFIED, NOT ASSUMED: read modes.py's 8 PROCEEDINGS definitions + params/contest.md Proceeding Types + social_contest_v30.md Sec2 Step 5 / Sec7 against each proceeding's THEMATIC purpose before picking a fix. Church Tribunal is the strongest thematic candidate: params/contest.md Proceeding Types describes it as 'Inquisitor proposes | Halved for accused | Expert Judge' -- an ACCUSATORIAL proceeding; social_contest_v30.md Sec7 'Church Tribunal specifics: Accused has no corroboration ... Church boosts Obscuring -- the Inquisitor's arguments that foreclose the accused's epistemic standing carry institutional weight' -- the proceeding's whole thematic shape is an Inquisitor INVESTIGATING WHETHER THE ACCUSED COMMITTED AN ACT (an evidentiary/conjectural question -- 'did X happen? was X done?', social_contest_v30.md Sec2 Step 2's own Memory-genre question-shape), not judging the wisdom of an already-settled matter (the qualitative terrain the other 7 open on: Royal Audience is a petition FOR something, Guild Arbitration is a dispute between equals before a bench, Formal/Grand Contest are parliamentary persuasion, Casual Dispute/Private Negotiation/Personal Appeal are informal with no institutional fact-finding role). wrapper.py's own GAMES registry independently corroborates this reading: the future game='inquiry' STUB row already cites 'Church Tribunal / Inquisition (author-new -- later stage)' as its canonical source, i.e. Church Tribunal was ALREADY understood elsewhere in this codebase as the fact-finding/investigative proceeding, before this ED. THE FIX (minimal, surgical, single proceeding): sim/personal/contest/modes.py -- added CHURCH_TRIBUNAL_START_GROUND = Stasis.FACT and a start_ground=CHURCH_TRIBUNAL_START_GROUND field on the church_tribunal PROCEEDINGS spec; modes.proceeding_venue() now reads an optional start_ground field off the proceeding spec and passes it to Venue(...) when the caller has not already supplied one (absent -> unchanged resolver.Venue default, Stasis.QUALITY, for the other 7 proceedings). No new down-shift mechanic was invented (stasis still only ever shifts upward, unchanged) and no other Church Tribunal field changed (exchanges (1,5), roles inquisitor_proposes, resistance halved_accused, adjudicator expert_judge, track_start 6.0 -- all unchanged). VERIFIED WITH A SEEDED TEST built via the normal wrapper.build_contest path (not a synthetic Bout bypassing the proceeding-derived stasis start): sim/personal/contest/_kernel_tests.py new 'CR4 REACHABILITY FIX' block confirms (a) proceeding_venue('church_tribunal').start_ground == Stasis.FACT and the other 6 non-Panel proceedings + guild_arbitration (Panel win-condition) are unchanged at Stasis.QUALITY; (b) church_tribunal's other fields are unchanged; (c) build_contest(5, 4, venue='church_tribunal').venue.start_ground == Stasis.FACT (the real wrapper path); (d) a real seeded Bout built from that Contest's own venue/adjudicator shows a Precedent (Memory-chosen) orator's mean track advantage differs from a Vision (Projection-chosen) orator's on the identical Church Tribunal proceeding, isolating the CR4 +1D as the only difference (zero armature_position); (e) a direct primary_genre_pool_bonus check on the Contest's own live ground confirms Precedent earns the +1D and Vision does not. +7 kernel checks (377 -> 384 total). CANON PROPAGATION (this stage never wrote to params/ or designs/scene/ -- done now, canon leads code follows): CR4 (Ciceronian stasis grounding the genre bonus, including the FULL corrected reachability fix), CR5 (self-Face backfire alongside the Doubt Marker), and the adjudicator armature (the STYLE_AXIS dot-product, the continuous delta-sigma channel via the CR6 'setup advantages accumulate as delta-sigma, tanh soft-capped' chain -- the SAME channel used for Recall/Face/corroboration/prep/commit-spend, not an abbreviated citation -- the asymmetric gate-off, the ratified 4th-axis decision, and the Appraise PARTIAL-reveal boundary) are now propagated into designs/scene/social_contest_v30.md (Sec2 Step 2 stasis->genre table + reachability note, Sec2 Step 5 proceeding table Stasis-Start column, Sec4 Step 3 Adjudicator Armature block, Sec4 Step 4 CR5 self-backfire block, Sec7 Church Tribunal FACT-stasis note, Sec8 Derived Values Summary CR4/CR5/Armature block) + its co-filed social_contest_v30_index.md (regenerated: heading/line/token table + canonical_sha updated, no heading added/removed) and into params/contest.md (Genre and Orientation Bonus Dice CR4 block, Proceeding Types Stasis Start column + reachability note, Interaction Types CR5 block, Face and Rattled header/note updated from '[UNREACHABLE UNTIL STAGE 3]' to '[PARTIALLY REACHABLE -- Stage 3 / Gate C, ED-1062]' honestly distinguishing the now-wired CR5 self-Face strip channel from the still-unwired general strain->Rattled threshold mechanic, and the CR1/CR2/CR3 Substrate header extended to CR1-CR5 + Armature + Epideictic-compression). params/contest.md has no co-filed index/infill pair, so no further co-file regeneration is required there. RATIFIED-COMMENT UPDATES (find every OPEN-DECISION hedge on these 4 items and state the ratification plainly, per the task): sim/personal/contest/armature.py (module STATUS docstring; ArmatureAxis class docstring + INSINUATION class attribute comment; the NB-on-the-4th-axis block comment above ArmatureAxis rewritten to 'RATIFIED (Jordan, Gate C, 2026-07-02) ...'; the STYLE_AXIS table comment + insinuation row comment); sim/personal/contest/rhetoric.py (module header CR5 block gains a 'CR5 SCOPE -- RATIFIED' paragraph; the EPIDEICTIC_COMPRESSION dict's 'decision_for_jordan' field + the module-header epideictic paragraph rewritten from 'OPEN: ...'/'Flagged as an open decision' to 'RATIFIED (Jordan, Gate C, 2026-07-02): ACCEPT ...'; the CR5_ORIENTATION_CHANNEL block comment + orientation_channel() docstring rewritten from 'DEFERRED (open item for Jordan)' framing to 'RATIFIED ... realized via the Doubt Marker + self-Face backfire together'; CR5_SELF_GATING['scope'] and ['open_forks_for_jordan'][a] rewritten from DEFERRED/OPEN framing to RATIFIED/RESOLVED framing (sub-items (b) and (c) -- the exact Face-cost-anchor quantity and a deeper standing-scaling variant -- remain genuinely open, unrelated to this ratification, and are NOT touched); sim/personal/contest/wrapper.py (MECHANICS['adjudicator_armature']['source'] and ['cr5_self_gating']['source'] hedge phrases '[STYLE_AXIS magnitudes + Insinuation axis = Jordan fork]' and 'CR5's Indirect->OPPONENT-Face-attack half is DEFERRED' rewritten to state the Gate C ratifications plainly); sim/personal/contest/_kernel_tests.py (the STYLE_AXIS / Insinuation-row test comments and assertion strings, the CR5 orientation-channel test comments and assertion strings, and the epideictic test comment/assertion updated to match the ratified language and assert on 'ratified'/'RATIFIED' markers instead of 'invention'/'DEFERRED' markers -- kept the suite passing at 384/384, not just the prose). Numeric weights (STYLE_AXIS_PRIMARY/OFFAXIS) remain explicitly [SEED] throughout -- the ratification is scoped to STRUCTURE (which 4th axis, whether the 2-genre compression stands, whether CR5's two halves are complementary), not to the un-calibrated magnitudes, per the task's explicit instruction not to treat those as separately blocked. description: FIELD_MOVEMENT default flip EXECUTED (Stage A step 7, Jordan-ratified 2026-07-02: "yes, field movement is default."): FIELD_MOVEMENT (hierarchy/units.py) and its prerequisite PC_NODE_COHESION (config.py) default 0 -> 1; the coordinate field is now what a bare invocation of the mass-battle engine runs. The integer grid remains fully available (FIELD_MOVEMENT=0 PC_NODE_COHESION=0) and stays the frozen byte-exact regression oracle: tests/valoria/test_mass_battle_byte_exact.py updated to PIN the grid toggles explicitly to their OFF values (the prior env.pop() approach would silently have run the grid-oracle check on the field path after the flip); both grid digests re-verified byte-identical with explicit pins. bat.py field golden digests (unit_field/cell_field) re-recorded -- the prior values were stale (recorded before the LC-8 battery migration, ED-1088) and the re-record also folds in ED-1091's frontal-only recoil gate. Workbench/server.py and index.html mode-banner docs updated for the inverted defaults. Known and accepted at ratification: the field path's historical-gauge pass-band still trails the grid baseline (documented since Stage A step 5; attributed to the unbuilt Stage D+ control-hierarchy depth, not to the substrate) -- ratifying the default acknowledges the field substrate as the live game's movement model per the 2026-07-02 session directive "we aren't using grids anymore."

Videogame sub-unit cap RULED (Jordan 2026-07-02: "subunits can be as high as 11."): the TTRPG hard cap of 3 on simultaneously-commanded sub-units (mass_battle_v30.md sA.5 Command Rating, PP-504/ED-899 -- a tabletop-bookkeeping limit) is lifted for the videogame surface; the hard ceiling is 11, enforced at construction by engine.build_army (ValueError above 11; verified: 11 constructs, 12 raises). Command remains the span-of-control governor (max simultaneous commanded = Command) WITHIN the ceiling, per sA.5 unchanged. OPEN RECONCILIATION (flagged in the sA.5 banner, deliberately not resolved here): the ratified Command formula clamps 1..7, so fielding more than 7 commanded sub-units implies a future Command-exceeding mechanism (e.g. subordinate officers/lieutenancy) -- a future ED, not silently invented in the constructor. Also noted: build_army's convenience default stagger (col 15+4i) walks off the 50-grid above ~8 subunits; explicit starting_position (the intended use for large armies) is unaffected.

Charge-recoil frontal zone-gate EXECUTED (Jordan 2026-07-02: "c7 if it is historically valid" -- condition verified against references/historical/mass_battle_gauge_grounding.md s4.3, the latent flag carried since 2026-06-16: "Historically a brace cannot repel what it cannot face (Burkholder)... Fix candidate: gate the recoil on the frontal (GREEN) octagon zone"). New PC_RECOIL_FRONTAL toggle (config.py, default ON; OFF reproduces the prior any-direction recoil byte-exactly): the reciprocal charge-recoil (PC_CHARGE_RECOIL) now fires only when the braced wall's per-cell-averaged octagon zone vs the charger is GREEN (frontal), using the same zone midpoint re-binning as the adjacent charge-shock path. Verified: grid cell digest byte-identical (the battery's only braced row is frontal); frontal braced charge still recoils (36 firings, unchanged behavior); an enveloping-cavalry-vs-braced-hold-line scenario (the exact configuration gauge row C7 deliberately avoided because of this bug) shows the gate suppressing ~26% of recoil firings (804 vs 1088 across 8 seeds) -- the flank/rear hits. Residual: gauge row C7 can now legitimately be extended with a braced+enveloped variant; grounding doc s4.3/s5.7 should be updated from "flagged, not fixed" on a future gauge pass.

Stage F (mass-battle) investigation + D2 fidelity-fix verification (no code changed -- this ED records an audit finding, not a behavior change). Investigated how much of the plan's Stage F ("Charge/Depth/Equipment Physics") is already implemented before writing new physics code, per this repo's own discipline against duplicating existing mechanics or inventing ungrounded magnitudes. Findings: (1) "Speed differential punctures" -- DONE, _momentum_speed (orchestration.py) feeds real per-cell speed into puncture (a_pen/b_pen), PC_CAVALRY_SPEED_MULT gates the velocity differential to cavalry/mounted_archers. (2) "Depth absorbs" -- DONE, a_pen/b_pen already reduced by _defender_depth (orchestration.py ~L697-698). (3) Fidelity D1 ("zone+actor-gate the recoil", designs/audit/2026-06-30-massbattle-bottomup/05_redesign_workplan.md :112) -- the ZONE half done this session (ED-1091, PC_RECOIL_FRONTAL); the ACTOR half (should the recoil require the charging side to actually BE cavalry/charging, not just have marginally higher per-tick momentum than a braced defender) remains OPEN -- no canonical predicate for "actor-gate" exists in any design doc; the 05_redesign_workplan.md anchor names the fix but not its mechanical definition. NOT invented here -- flagged for Jordan's ruling. (4) Fidelity D2 ("gate cavalry high-lethality on regime: flank/rear OR shaken OR completed envelopment -> high; frontal-vs-steady -> low", same doc :113-114) -- VERIFIED ALREADY CORRECT by direct numeric probe of the existing, unmodified _charge_shock_sigma (resolution.py) with representative inputs: frontal+braced -0.0101, frontal+unbraced -0.0289, flank(YELLOW) -0.1925, rear(RED) -0.308, frontal+shaken -0.0481, rear+shaken(worst case) -0.5133 -- the exact stated ordering (frontal-steady lowest, rear+shaken highest, ~50x spread), using the mechanism and constants exactly as they already stand -- no value was tuned or invented to produce this result, matching the mass_battle_gauge_grounding.md precedent of VERIFYING differentiation via direct probe rather than asserting it. D2 closed. (5) "PC_CHARGE_* derived, not asserted" -- NOT DONE; correctly out of Stage F's own scope, already tracked as Track M Stage 5 (the engine-wide calibrated-debt retirement sweep) in designs/audit/2026-06-30-massbattle-bottomup/05_redesign_workplan.md, not something to fold into Stage F piecemeal. Also confirmed (2)-(6) via full-text review: designs/audit/2026-06-23-mb-fidelity-critique/README.md independently anchors D1 as still-open and its own Phase-0 deliverables (register.md/ners_verdict.md/reconciliation.md) do not yet exist. ED-1080 (HANDOFF.md "grounded combat re-baseline") confirmed UNRELATED -- it is the scene/personal 1v1-duel combat engine (weapon_physics.py percussion/armour/gap-game), a different system.

Cross-scale propagation spec v1 authored and ratified (governed by ED-1094 merge-ratifies-by-default like any other PR -- no carve-out invoked; flip pre-staged in this same PR per CLAUDE.md Section 2's 'as part of the same merge, not a separate later step' rule -- the exact gap that left ED-1083 stranded PROPOSED after PR #55 merged): designs/architecture/propagation_spec_v1.md -- the workplan v5 J-38 docket item the holonic doctrine (ED-1083) named as the highest-value unauthored canon and explicitly declined to author itself. Supplies: (1) the aggregate-up transform (Domain Echo -> substrate write / decaying national-event-modifier Key, never the aggregate itself -- F1/IV.2-law-2 compliant); (2) the distribute-down transform (formalizes scale_transitions_v30.md Section 12.3 authoring discipline, closes the eight Section 12.4 down-seams, one-Key-N-targets fan-out rule -- Section 12.2 compliant, no bespoke channel); (3) the ordering/determinism spine, supplying engine_clock's home doc pending ED-1051 (module-contract doc:null closure, a separate still-open editorial item this ratification does not by itself resolve) -- season-is-the-tick, SEASON_TICK/ACTION/ACCOUNTING_BOUNDARY phases, sub_step_index assignment rule; (4) a termination guarantee (per-tick fixpoint Theorem A, per-cascade termination Theorem B via a cascade_depth counter, plus a proven per-hop magnitude bound) that is explicitly TERMINATION-ONLY -- cross-tick CONVERGENCE is named as NOT proven, conditional on an unspecified decay() being strictly contractive and on a down/up double-count (Distribute-Down D.6) being ruled disjoint. AUTHORSHIP PROCESS (two adversarial rounds): four grounded producers drafted the sections; three independent adversarial critics found real fatal/major holes in the first pass (a single field, sub_step_index, doing two incompatible jobs -- append-order tiebreak vs cascade-depth meter; an uncapped accounting-phase emission cascade; a live-vs-deferred echo-application timing contradiction between the aggregate-up wiring and the termination section's own requirement); a repair pass closed all of them with real mechanism changes; two independent re-verification passes confirmed the repair holds. A SEPARATE whole-corpus Fable-tier review then found further issues even after that: the doc had wrongly presented Stability Crisis's perpetual-scene risk as an OPEN Jordan decision when scale_transitions_v30.md Section 4.3.2 already rules it via ED-749 hysteresis (edge-triggered, 2-Accounting re-arm, player's faction only) -- retired to OF-HYSTERESIS-AUDIT (the narrower, genuinely-open question: do OTHER Section 4.3.2 triggers have an ED-749-equivalent rule; sim/cross_scale/scene_dispatch.py not complying with ED-749 is a sim bug, not a spec decision); also found an uncited RNG-model collision against key_substrate_v30.md Section 6.1's per-emission-seed contract (flagged RNG-MODEL-COLLISION, not resolved) and a self-contradictory ratification-posture note (fixed). PROPOSES two real, cited amendments to key_substrate_v30.md (OF-7: Section 4.1 step-4 deferred-apply-target extension, armed with a competitor-analysis defense against the cheaper Drafted-state alternative; OF-B1: Section 4.1 step-5 tightened from 'O(1) OR async' to 'no synchronous re-entry') -- both flagged for Jordan/an ED ticket, NOT applied to key_substrate_v30.md itself. Consolidated decision queue (Section 5 of the doc) ranks the open items: OF-7/OF-B1 amendments first (the guarantee's own legal footing), then the D.6 double-count (HIGH PRIORITY, confirmed live driver of non-convergence), then RNG-MODEL-COLLISION, decay() specification, OF-HYSTERESIS-AUDIT, cap constants (CASCADE_DEPTH_MAX/EMISSIONS_PER_TICK_MAX, unbacked, no fabrication -- verified absent from the tree as any assigned numeric value), and the ORD-3/ORD-4 determinism preconditions (compute_observers order-preservation in key_substrate_v30.md; scene_slate._queue module-global fix). Explicitly does NOT reopen scale_transitions_v30.md Section 12's downward-delivery ruling (J-1/ED-1038) or ratify the faction-stat-inversion aggregate formula (register #3/R6, still OPEN). RATIFIED as written 2026-07-02 -- ratification covers the document's own explicit PROVEN-vs-NOT-PROVEN scope markers and every open flag listed above; it does not itself resolve any of them.

Merge-approval-ratifies-by-default governance convention adopted (Jordan). Prior convention (implicit, established this session authoring ED-1083) treated a PROPOSED design doc as surviving a PR merge unchanged -- merging landed the work in the tree but did not itself flip PROPOSED/provisional status to CANONICAL/ratified, requiring a separate explicit sign-off. This produced a live confusion: PR #55 was reviewed and merged by Jordan, but designs/architecture/holonic_container_doctrine_v1.md (ED-1083) remained tagged PROPOSED/provisional in main afterward, and decision_queue.md item 20 still listed its own ratification as an open Jordan decision despite the merge having already happened. RULING (Jordan, 2026-07-02): merging a PR ratifies everything PROPOSED/provisional within it BY DEFAULT. Any item that genuinely needs separate, explicit sign-off beyond ordinary merge review MUST be called out loudly in the PR body as held back -- it must not ride silently inside a routine-work PR and rely on a later, separate ratification step nobody triggers. ACTION taken same-day: ED-1083 flipped provisional -> ratified under this convention (see its entry); holonic_container_doctrine_v1.md Status line flipped PROPOSED -> CANONICAL; CURRENT.md gained an Architecture/Doctrine row; decision_queue.md item 20 struck as resolved; CLAUDE.md Section 2 documents the standing rule for future sessions. SCOPE: this convention governs future PRs; it does not retroactively re-open already-closed decisions, and does not apply to items a PR body explicitly flags as held back (e.g. the holonic doctrine itself never claimed to ratify the propagation-spec docket, J-38 -- that remains a separate authorship task, gated on its own review, not on any past merge). RENUMBERED TWICE on 2026-07-02 by the same concurrent-allocation race: originally filed as ED-1088, collided with origin/main's unrelated ED-1088 (mass-battle LC-8, PR #59) and was renumbered to ED-1090; then origin/main independently landed THREE MORE mass-battle EDs (ED-1089/1090/1091, PR #62; ED-1092, PR #65) while this session's branch was still in flight holding its own ED-1089 (propagation spec) and ED-1090 (this entry) -- renumbered again, this entry to ED-1094 and the propagation spec to ED-1093 (see its entry). id_reservations.yaml's disjoint-block protocol prevents cross-lane collisions only when every lane actually reads next_free before allocating; two same-session collisions on one block is the signal that the flat ED-NNNN sequence itself, not just the protocol's discipline, is the bottleneck -- flagged to Jordan as motivation for a lane-tagged ID namespace (e.g. ED-MB-####, ED-SC-####, ED-IN-####) to make cross-lane collision structurally impossible rather than retryable; scoping deferred, not decided here. Precedent: ED-1080 was similarly renumbered from ED-1055 on a prior collision.

Charge-recoil actor/timing/reach gating EXECUTED per Jordan's multi-part 2026-07-02 ruling (verbatim: "Recoil should require the charging to actually be cavalry/sprinting-charging, not just being faster. ... the ability for a defender to brace is governed by their ability to set up their formation earlier...which likely requires a tick. ... if a faster side has longer reach, they would still have a reach advantage over a braced formation... Bracing is not something that can be done instantaneously, but must be prepared ahead of time and intentionally set. ... mounted archers shouldn't be closing in on the enemy--they should be flying by and taking advantage of their range advantage with their bows."). Four parts, all additive/toggle-gated (default ON; OFF reproduces pre-ruling behavior byte-exactly): T1 (actor-gate) -- new PC_RECOIL_CHARGER_GATE (config.py) requires the charging atom's troop_type=='cavalry' literally (mounted_archers excluded, see T4) before the reciprocal charge-recoil (PC_CHARGE_RECOIL) fires, threaded into orchestration.resolve_engagements's existing recoil conditional. T2 (brace-setup delay) -- new PC_BRACE_SETUP_DELAY (config.py) + Subunit._brace_since_tick (hierarchy/units.py, stamped 0 at construction if deployed already braced, stamped to the firing tick by core/contact.check_orders when an Order adds 'brace' mid-battle, reset to -1 on removal) require >=1 full tick of continuous brace before resolution._subunit_braced/_unit_braced (both now t-parameterized, t=None preserves the old instantaneous check) treat a subunit as braced for either the charge-shock defensive benefit or the reciprocal recoil; t threaded through resolve_engagements/resolve_engagements_cascading/run_battle's call site. T3 (reach-gate) -- PC_RECOIL_CHARGER_GATE additionally requires the defender's reach_for(troop_type) >= the charger's (troop_types.registry.reach_for, the existing Stage-A primitive) -- structural-only today since TROOP_TYPE_REACH stays deliberately empty (everyone defaults REACH_SHORT), so this half is a no-op pending a separate troop-type-to-reach-class ruling; not populated speculatively per the evidentiary-primitive discipline. T4 (mounted-archer default kiting) -- engine.build_army: a spec with troop_type=='mounted_archers' and no explicit role/shape/instructions implicitly defaults role='Kite' (explicit caller choice of any of the three always wins); build_unit unchanged (no 'unspecified shape' case exists there). Verified: bat.py --check byte-exact across all 4 digest modes (unit/cell/unit_field/cell_field) against the frozen baselines; tests/valoria pytest suite 81 passed, 10 skipped, unchanged.

Mass-battle movement/pathing audit RATIFIED (Fable-5-led, Opus-adversarially-verified, 16 subagents, zero errors, all findings CONFIRMED or PARTIALLY_CONFIRMED, zero REFUTED). Full report: designs/audit/2026-07-02-mass-battle-movement-pathing-audit/README.md + findings_full.json. Root cause of Jordan's 'wings just go straight to the opponent, no wheeling/circling' observation: since ED-1089 (FIELD_MOVEMENT/PC_NODE_COHESION default ON), every battle runs Subunit._node_advance (hierarchy/units.py:554-679), a pure straight-line centroid attractor that never reads instructions/role. All four real maneuver behaviors (envelop wrap-to-rear, sweep flank-march, overhang wheel, ranged kite) exist only on the legacy grid path (units.py:719-861), unreachable via an unconditional early return -- the maneuvers aren't missing, they're unreachable in the default configuration. Ten further findings (critical: reset_positions is a no-op on node state so multi-turn battles freeze at the turn-1 contact line for turns 2-8; ED-1095's mounted-archer kiting fix is NOT effective, role='Kite' never sets unit_type='ranged' so the unit neither kites nor volleys on any path -- high/medium/low: dead v12 column-holding causing wing convergence, maneuver acceptance validators measuring the dead path since ED-1089, formation-drift node-state corruption, PER_CELL still OFF by default compiling out charge-shock/brace-recoil/cavalry-speed, silently discarded unknown build_army spec keys, direction-dependent node movement speed, mislabeled ROLE_SPEC instruction-consumer count, once-per-battle order firing). Two corrections to earlier same-session claims: the '144-tick total freeze' empirical finding is REFUTED (does not reproduce at HEAD or 3 prior commits; likely a measurement bug reading the immutable cell-id field instead of live position); the reset_positions 'teleports to spawn every turn' characterization was imprecise -- it is a no-op on the node path (positions freeze at wherever they ended, not reset to spawn), a different failure mode with the same 'no dynamic mid-battle adjustment' symptom Jordan flagged. Also carries a Jordan-ruled path-length budget formula for the forthcoming waypoint primitive (0.5 x speed x max-ticks-in-battle, undeliverable paths rejected at design time not silently truncated) and confirms two general maneuver capability requirements from Jordan's annotated reference screenshots (wide-arc-then-cut-in-from-flank; asymmetric per-wing shapes with an interior strike point between friendly bodies) that the current single-point-attractor architecture cannot express without one new primitive (an ordered per-subunit goal/predicate waypoint list, modeled on the legacy envelop's existing two-state machine). An 8-step, dependency-ordered fix plan and 4 Jordan decision gates are recorded in the audit doc. Byte-exact CI grid digests are unaffected by any of this (every finding/fix is inside the node path, never entered when toggles are OFF); bat.py field digests will need a deliberate re-record once fixes land. Implementation NOT started -- this ED records the audit/ratification only, per Jordan's routing ruling (Fable proposes, Sonnet 5 writes).

ED-<LANE>-NNNN lane-tagged editorial namespace created (Jordan). Motivated by TWO same-session concurrent-allocation collisions on the flat ED-NNNN sequence within one PR (#58): first ED-1088 collided with an independently-merged mass-battle ED-1088 (LC-8, PR #59), renumbered to ED-1090; then ED-1089/1090 collided AGAIN with three more mass-battle EDs independently landed via PR #62/#65, renumbered to ED-1093/1094 (see ED-1094's own entry for the full incident). The existing references/id_reservations.yaml A-E block scheme only prevents collision through allocation DISCIPLINE (read next_free before allocating, retry on races); two same-session collisions on one active block in a single PR is a real signal that flat-numeric blocks alone do not scale to genuinely concurrent multi-session work on this repo. RULING (Jordan): new EDs use ED-<LANE>-NNNN, a 2-letter lane code + 4-digit zero-padded sequence, making cross-lane collision impossible BY CONSTRUCTION rather than by retryable discipline. Lane roster (9): MB mass battle, PC personal combat, FI field investigation, SC social contest, FA faction actions, WR world, IN infrastructure/cross-cutting, GO godot, SE settlements -- SC (social contest) and PC (personal/scene combat) were deliberately disambiguated after the first draft proposed SC for both; PY (python) was proposed and then dropped as not a real lane (a language, not a subsystem). MIGRATION: new-IDs-only -- the flat ED-NNNN sequence is FROZEN as of this entry (verified_live_max.ED=1094, no new flat allocations) but remains permanently valid; none of the existing ~1094 entries are retrofitted. Both formats resolve through the same ledger/citation-audit path forever. IMPLEMENTATION: references/id_reservations.yaml gained a lane_ids section (per-lane next_free counters, all starting at 1 except IN which starts at 2 -- this entry is ED-IN-0001, the cutover's own first-ever lane-tagged id, allocated to itself); tools/validate_ed_citations.py's CITE_RE/build_status_map/_walk_entries extended to recognize ED-<LANE>-NNNN alongside flat ED-NNNN (both fold through the same _canon_id normalization, zero-padding-insensitive); tools/currency_consistency_check.py gained check_lane_id_ceilings (per-lane ceiling drift, mirroring the existing flat check_id_ceilings, deliberately independent since the flat ceiling is now frozen); tools/atomizer.py and tools/index_gen.py flagged (not fixed -- low priority, atomizer's archival chunking won't hit lane-tagged volume for a long time; index_gen is already SUPERSEDED/dead) with known-gap comments rather than silent breakage. CLAUDE.md Section 3 documents the format contract and a NEW, NOT YET CI-ENFORCED convention: a session should declare its lane (via the ED-<LANE> ids it allocates) and keep commits/PRs scoped to that lane's files, except genuinely cross-cutting IN work or cross-lane-collision resolution. Mechanical enforcement of the scoping rule (detecting which lane a PR's file changes belong to and flagging mismatches) is explicitly NOT built here -- flagged as real follow-up work, not invented as a fuzzy heuristic gate under time pressure. Tests: 5 new cases in tests/hooks/test_ed_citation_integrity.py (lane-tagged parsing, padding, NONEXISTENT/OPEN_AS_BASIS, flat/lane-tagged non-collision, unrecognized-lane-code non-match) + 4 new cases in tests/valoria/test_currency_consistency_check.py (lane-max extraction ignoring flat ids, ceiling drift, missing-lane flagging, clean-when-empty) -- all green alongside the full existing suite.

Movement/pathing audit (ED-1096) fix-plan EXECUTED end-to-end (steps 1-7 + decision gates 2/4) plus a 5-dimension adversarial-review pass, on the mass-battle lane's designated branch claude/mass-battle-audit-5c6nih. First allocation on this lane under the ED-<LANE>-NNNN namespace (ED-IN-0001) -- prior in-code/in-doc citations of 'ED-1097' throughout this work (units.py, config.py, bat.py, engine.py, gauge_mb.py, equipment/__init__.py, troop_types/registry.py, test_mass_battle_maneuvers.py, coverage_matrix.md) predate this branch learning of the cutover and are RENUMBERED to this ID; ED-1095/1096 (filed earlier this session, before the cutover) are unaffected and remain valid flat IDs (see id_reservations.yaml's verified_live_max note). ED-1096 established the root cause: since the coordinate field became default (ED-1089), 'envelop'/'sweep'/wheel maneuver instructions only ever worked on the legacy grid path (advance_cells) -- the live default node path (_node_advance) was a pure straight-line centroid attractor that ignored them entirely. FIX-PLAN STEPS EXECUTED: (1) Subunit._rekey_node_state fixes check_drift's node-state corruption on a mid-battle shape change. (2) orchestration.reset_positions made a deliberate no-op for node-path atoms (position genuinely does not reset between engagement turns within a battle, per Jordan). Decision gate 2 (weapon-derived unit_type): troop_types.registry.unit_type_for derives unit_type from the troop's assigned weapon (equipment.TROOP_LOADOUT/loadout_for), wired into build_unit/build_army; kite's steering gate decoupled from unit_type=='ranged' (kite is weapon-independent per Jordan, best with a bow but executable by lance cavalry -- only volley fire itself still requires ranged). (4) Restored the v12 lateral file-holding term in _node_advance (a sibling in a multi-subunit Unit holds its own deployment file instead of collapsing onto the shared centroid column). (5) Fixed the node WHEEL's 180-degree facing stall (lerp-normalize replaced with a rotation-based update). (6) Re-pointed the maneuver acceptance validators (validators.py's v_envelop/v_sweep) at the node path via a new path parameter and _set_movement_path, landed as tests/valoria/test_mass_battle_maneuvers.py. (7) Added the waypoint primitive -- Subunit._resolve_maneuver_goal/_envelop_goal/_sweep_goal -- giving _node_advance a per-tick, live-geometry-derived anchor goal for 'envelop'/'sweep', modeled on the legacy per-cell two-state machine at anchor granularity. Decision gate 4 (PER_CELL default flip): config.py's PER_CELL default '0'->'1' (same ED-1089 precedent), unlocking charge shock/ brace recoil/cavalry speed/fatigue/the ED-1091/ED-1095 gates by default; found and fixed two latent 'independently re-derive PER_CELL from a stale hardcoded os.environ default' bugs in bat.py and gauge_mb.py that would have silently mislabeled/misgated a bare invocation the moment the config default changed. DECISION GATES 1 AND 3 EXPLICITLY DEFERRED (Jordan: sequencing ruling, gates 1/3 occur AFTER pathing/routing is confirmed working) -- Command/Discipline-gated conditional tactics and the facing/attention split are NOT built here, by design. ADVERSARIAL REVIEW (5-dimension Workflow, sonnet finders + opus verify, per Jordan's 'proceed that way re workflow' instruction): 8 findings, 6 CONFIRMED + 1 PLAUSIBLE fixed same session (kite steering never ported to the node path -- a mounted_archers subunit correctly got instructions=('kite',...) from gate 2 but fell through to plain centroid steering and closed to melee, the exact bug class this whole audit exists to fix, for a 4th instruction the first pass missed, closed via a new _kite_goal; an escort atom's column-tracking was silently overridden by the sibling-column-holding fallback the instant its escorted unit's column diverged, defeating Stage C's own escort machinery; a test fixture leak; a dead constructor parameter converted to an assertion; a unit_type=None sentinel inconsistency between build_unit/build_army; two documentation-provenance corrections), 1 REFUTED (a real but non-defect test-coverage gap, flagged not fixed). VERIFICATION: all 4 bat.py digest modes (unit/cell grid, unit_field/cell_field node) byte-exact throughout every step against their respective baselines (2 unchanged grid, 2 deliberately re-recorded field -- isolated via bisection to steps 1/4/5/7, not a regression); full tests/valoria suite green (88 passed/10 skipped/1 xfailed after this branch merged origin/main's ED-IN-0001/0002 infrastructure). Functional: a build_envelopment wing tracked tick-by-tick via workbench/trace.py under DEFAULT toggles wheeled from its starting flank (row 36) to row 19 -- past the defender's own settled row (24.6) -- while holding its own flank column, a genuine double-envelopment geometry, not a straight walk-in; delivered to Jordan as an interactive tick-by-tick Artifact re-tracing the exact H4 (Envelopment vs Arrowhead) scenario shown broken earlier in the engagement. gauge_mb.py re-run under PER_CELL=1: single=2/20, multi=4/20. DISCLOSED, NOT CHASED (per this repo's standing discipline against retuning magnitudes to fit a band): a combat-PACING interaction where PER_CELL's newly-live fatigue/attrition/envelopment-sigma mechanics resolve a frontal engagement faster than a wide detour can complete -- confirmed non-movement via direct isolation (forcing orchestration.PER_CELL=False alone restores test_envelop_reaches_rear_node's pass; an isolated single-subunit fight at the same troop ratio favors the attacker 14-0-6/20 seeds; varying travel distance barely moved the outcome) and visible at battle-composition scale in the gauge re-run (H3/H4(Cannae)/H5/H6 lose 0-13% instead of the expected 45-72% band, consistent with an envelopment composition's pinning center routing before its wings complete the maneuver). Landed as a loud, documented xfail(strict=False) with the full diagnostic chain in its own docstring; the underlying combat-balance/pacing question (numerically-dominant vs. thin-and-yielding pinning force; a maneuver time-budget separate from the frontal fight's own clock) is flagged for whoever next works PER_CELL=1 combat balance -- out of this movement/pathing fix's scope. Full detail: tests/coverage_matrix.md's 2026-07-02 movement/pathing-audit entries; designs/audit/2026-07-02-mass-battle-movement-pathing-audit/README.md.

description: Mass-battle Cannae gauge-band root-cause audit RATIFIED (merged via PR #73, ED-1094 merge-ratifies-by-default). Follow-up to ED-MB-0001's disclosed-not-chased finding that PER_CELL's combat mechanics collapse the H3-H6 Cannae-pattern historical gauge rows from a passing baseline to 0-13% losses against a 45-72% band. Produced by a Fable-led Workflow (4 parallel research agents; 4 independent adversarial diagnostic lenses -- clock_coupling, historical_fidelity, alternative_mechanism, design_options; each cross-critiqued by an independent Fable pass; Fable reconciliation re-verifying every contested fact against the working tree before adjudicating; a final Sonnet pass authoring the fix-plan document). RANKED ROOT CAUSE: demotes this session's own prior 'two racing clocks' hypothesis (test_envelop_reaches_rear_node's docstring) -- refuted as a general/gauge-scale account by gauge row C7 (a genuinely multi-subunit cavalry envelopment) passing its band, though it survives narrowly at the original diagnostic fixture's own scope. RC-1 (VERY HIGH confidence): three composition-coupling defects in the pool/morale accounting layer -- (a) a composed subunit's combat pool scales down per simultaneous-engagement pair while a single-subunit opponent rolls full pool into every pair (orchestration.py:504-528, POOL_VARIANT=C-ii); (b) two subunits' independent morale triggers double-erode one shared parent-Unit morale pool because build_army never populates a subunit's own morale field (hierarchy/units.py erode_morale/cohesion ~L404-431); (c) PER_CELL's casualty-fraction triggers use subunit-scale denominators (core/state.py:27-38). Plus two independent narrower bugs (a validator fixture ghost-cell construction-order bug, validators.py:205; a float-epsilon pool-floor bug, orchestration.py:527-528). RC-2 (HIGH confidence): the old PER_CELL=0 baseline that used to pass H3-H6 was itself an invincibility-bug artifact (multi-subunit cohesion structurally frozen at 1.0), not a working model to restore. RC-3 (MEDIUM, confounded): RC-1's couplings plausibly extend to gauge scale but this is not proven -- C4-vs-C7 (identical _envelop_army composition, one fails its band, one passes) is the sharpest unexplained datapoint. RC-4 (design gap, not a defect): the engine has no state between eroding and routed, so Hannibal's actual thin-and-yielding center tactic is currently inexpressible -- correctly deferred until RC-1 is fixed. RC-5 (LOW-MEDIUM, undiagnosed): 9 of 20 gauge rows fail for reasons unrelated to composition coupling at all -- flagged as a separate, not-yet-opened lane item. FIX PLAN (deliberately narrow, gate-free work only): 2 independent bug fixes safe to build now, 3 measurement/ablation steps (a per-coupling ablation harness; a gauge-scale frozen-wings ablation; a C4-vs-C7 mechanism trace) to close the remaining open questions -- explicitly does not build the accounting fix itself. DECISION GATES DG-3 and DG-4 RULED THE SAME DAY (recorded as an addendum in the audit doc, not re-litigated here): DG-3 = Option A, split the defender's pool symmetrically across pairs (Jordan's stated criterion: long-term integrity and fidelity to reality -- Option A reuses the existing pool-split mechanism symmetrically rather than inventing an underived engage-fraction formula (B) or a band-fit magnitude bump (C), and is the physically correct model besides). DG-4 = 'a blend of per-subunit as well as whole unit' morale (Jordan, verbatim) -- operationalized by wiring ALREADY-EXISTING, currently-inert machinery rather than authoring new state: hierarchy/units.py's agg_morale()/derive_rout() (~L1460-1481, troop-weighted aggregate for whole-unit rout) and cascade_morale_hit() (~L1482-1491, army-wide contagion) already implement the blend; the only gap is that build_army/build_envelopment/build_refused_flank never populate a subunit's own morale field at construction (confirmed by direct read, engine.py:243-246), so every subunit falls through to the shared parent pool. The fix is to default each subunit to its own real starting morale value at construction, closing the double-count by construction with zero new state. DG-1, DG-2, DG-5 remain OPEN, gated on the DG-3/DG-4 fix landing and the gauge being re-measured. Implementation of the fix plan + DG-3/DG-4 rulings NOT yet started as of this entry -- this ED records the audit's ratification and the two same-day gate rulings only.

Follow-up to ED-MB-0002: DG-3/DG-4 landed (PR #75) but did NOT close the H3-H6 Cannae-pattern gauge-band gap (draws unchanged). A fresh Fable-5-led adversarial audit of the shipped DG-3/DG-4 code found four concrete, reproducible engine defects surviving that fix, two introduced/made load-bearing by the DG-3 rewrite itself: D1, an outer troops_frac/army-size divisor applied to a subunit base_pool BEFORE pair_pool_contribution's own per-troop normalization, diluting a composed subunit's pool by its share of the WHOLE ARMY's troops rather than its own troop type/quality/density (orchestration.py, POOL_VARIANT=="C-ii"); D2, _envelop_goal's phase-1/phase-2 transition sharing one threshold for entry and exit with no hysteresis, producing a permanent limit cycle where a wheeling wing never actually reaches contact (hierarchy/units.py); D3, routed/broken atoms resurrected to combat pool 1 by an unconditional max(1,...) floor, contradicting SS A.12 "cannot fight back" (orchestration.py); D4, distribute_casualties tracking engaged columns as one union across a whole Unit, letting an uninvolved subunit (e.g. a far wing) absorb a different subunit's casualties by column coincidence (percell.py). Plus a harness-level force-ratio bug (gauge_mb.py's _envelop_army/_refused_army fielded 3x/2x a single-subunit opponent's troops, a silent side-effect of the LC-8 migration, making every DG-1 composition question untestable). JORDAN RULINGS (2026-07-05, via AskUserQuestion): DG-3 completion = "Intensive (per-troop, partition-invariant)" pool semantics -- the outer troops_frac multiplier removed entirely from the C-ii branch (pair_pool_contribution already normalizes per-troop internally). DG-1 = "symmetric at parity + majority pin cavalry wing so long as bottom-up emergent primitives approach" -- _envelop_army/_refused_army rebuilt at force parity (same total troops as their single-subunit opponent) via the continuous-scale troops/concentration path; infantry rows (H3/H5/H6) keep the symmetric center+2-wings shape at parity; cavalry rows (C4/C7) rebuilt as a majority (2/3) infantry pin + minority cavalry envelopment wings, matching the Polybius/Livy order of battle, via engine.build_envelopment unmodified. DG-2 (fighting-withdrawal/yield mechanic) = "create as workplan" -- NOT implemented this pass; captured as designs/proposals/mass_battle_fighting_withdrawal_v1.md, status PROPOSED. All four D1-D4 fixes + both ratified decisions implemented; an independent adversarial review found two of the six changes had real defects (D3's pool-zeroing was a no-op -- roll_pool/_sigma_net_boost both re-floor pool to a minimum of 1 internally regardless of the zeroed input, so the actual fix forces a_net/b_net to exactly 0 directly; gauge_mb.py's new wing_speed/speed kwargs never reached Unit.speed since Subunit has no speed field and it was never forwarded to build_envelopment/build_refused_flank) -- both fixed and re-verified. All 4 bat.py digest modes re-recorded (this touches shared, non-gated combat-resolution code, as ED-MB-0002's own fix did). tests/valoria 88 passed/16 skipped(numpy,unrelated)/1 xfailed. HONEST RESULT: draws are now GONE across H3/H4/H5/H6/C4 (were 100%/90%/100%/100%/66.7%), a genuine and dramatic change -- but these rows now OVERSHOOT their historical bands decisively in the attacker's favor (WIN-OUT) instead of landing inside them, except C7 which lands cleanly at the top of its wide band. Full 20-row gauge aggregate moved only marginally (4/20 -> 5/20 passing, multi mode n=30 -- H1,C1,C2,C6,C7; C1 newly passes). A controlled, isolated experiment (co-located vs spatially-separated equal-troop subunit splits, both vs an identical single-subunit opponent) suggests why: subunit_combat_pool's Command-driven combat score is not scaled by a subunit's own share of army-wide troops, so multiple spatially-separated attacking fronts against one defender can each roll close to a full, independent combat score simultaneously, tempered only by a small ENCIRCLEMENT_PENALTY tax that in this configuration falls on the defender, not the attacker. Whether this is a genuine partition-invariance defect needing its own fix, or the historically-correct emergent mechanism for why real encirclements are so devastating (with the bands themselves needing reconsideration), is explicitly NOT decided here -- flagged as a new, unresolved architecture question requiring its own dedicated Jordan ruling, not a silent magnitude tweak. RC-5's 9 unrelated single-subunit rows remain a separate, not-yet-opened lane item; DG-5 (racing-clocks) is corrected/re-confirmed closed (the original ablation's null result was itself confounded by the now-fixed D2 bug, not evidence of a genuinely settled non-race).
